profile.statement_to_voters:
I am a local resident who has lived in Southampton for 28 years. If elected my goal is to help the people of Coxford with issues you bring to me and represent those views to Southampton City Council.
As a Green, I aim to help make Southampton a Greener and more pleasant place to live.
As an engineer I have worked in industry and also run my own part time business, so will understand the issues around being self-employed. 
Being Green is about hope not hate as practiced by other parties. So let's move first and Vote Green.

''The system is set-up to line the pockets of the super-rich and to hell with the rest of us. We stand for what our communities need, starting with a fully funded NHS, kick out the private profiteers and full pay restoration for all staff.''

Its time for reforms. The uni party has failed us all and we need a fresh, new and invigorating Party in the form of Reform UK which puts families, communities and country first. We need to get back to basics, cut waste and focus on the peoples needs.

On May 7th 2026, I will be seeking your help once more to become Councillor for our area covering **Aldermoor, Coxford, Lordshill and Lordswood.**

In 2024 at the last attempt, it was my closest showing yet. My vote share was raised and I **was just 46 votes behind Labour, and over 750 votes ahead of all other parties.**

Over the last two years two of your councillors changed party. One from Labour to the Greens, who came fifth in 2024, and one from Labour to the Lib Dems, who came fourth in 2024. In that time you know I was still fighting for you.

As few examples of Action for Our Ward

- **Lordshill Housing Office** 
I have had success in removing the eyesore of the Lordshill Housing office. I demonstrated the yearly costs it was accruing while just a shell and had been left too long to be turned into a usable site again.

- **Coxford / Tremona Road Junction:** 
Rather than simply acknowledging issues, I found that the University and Hospital were redeveloping the site on the corner. Through my Conservative colleagues we got this raised at full council level to ask if the Council were seeking the ability to renew this junction that has the City’s most bus routes outside the City Centre

- **Exposing Wasteful Spending:** 
A further freedom of information request exposed the costs of the new City “welcome” signs that do nothing to demonstrate the history or gravitas of our standing as cruise capital.

- **Fighting to Restore Street Lighting:**
I’ve fought the switch-off since day one. By identifying incorrectly unlit pathways, I successfully got the lights turned back on in key areas. Now, I’m fighting to restore full lighting so our 24-hour workers can feel safe. You can still sign the petition: **www.change.org/24hlights4soton**

And finally of course, when any resident in need, I will seek to help and find a satisfactory resolution.

Give me the chance to be your councillor and you give me a platform to do more for every person in this ward, to push further for solutions for our ward. To ensure our ward has a person who is proactive in finding incoming issues and makes sure that you feel represented.

profile.statement_to_voters: https://lewes.greenparty.org.uk/our-priorities/

As a councillor and a previous Mayor of Lewes (the 9th female and the first BAME woman to hold that title), I know and love our area. That’s why I want to see the end to 19 years of Tory control at East Sussex County Council.

The Liberal Democrats are the second largest group on East Sussex County Council and the main challengers to the Conservatives in the elections this year across the county. I aim to successfully defend this seat to give us the best chance of taking control of the council away from the Conservatives.

I care deeply about our community, our environment, and our children, and, if elected, I will help challenge the injustices that have trapped many households in a vicious circle of disadvantage.

Standing up for our school children
Children only get one chance at their education. There is no greater investment than making sure every child gets the opportunity to fulfil their potential at school.

That means more money for education, especially in those vital early years, so that no child is left behind.

But the Conservatives are cutting school budgets in real terms despite promising more money during the General Election.

The Liberal Democrats are the party of education and opportunity. We will fight to make sure no school and no pupil misses out.

Standing up for our environment
East Sussex is a beautiful place to live, but our environment and landscape is under threat.

In Lewes, the Liberal Democrats are at the forefront of tackling climate change - taking real action, now. East Sussex County Council, under the Conservatives, is not doing nearly enough. Nationally, the Conservatives are loosening the planning system that protects our wonderful landscape. Locally, they are doing far too little to protect our environment.

If we control the county council we could and we would take positive action on the climate crisis.

Fixing our roads and pavements
Sussex’s residents have been badly let down by the Conservative-run county council.

After 20 years of Conservatives in charge of our roads and pavements they are potholed and in a state of disrepair. These potholes damage our cars, represent an accident risk for our cyclists and are a trip hazard for people using the pavements, especially our more elderly residents.

Lib Dems would ring-fence the money from parking charges to repair and maintain our pavements and roads, making them safer for car drivers, cyclists and walkers.

20 mph
As well as that, I am strongly supporting the campaign to secure 20 mph limits in our villages and residential areas.

profile.statement_to_voters:
The son of a Royal Marine, Bruce was widely travelled in his formative years.  As a consequence, he studied at ten schools before settling down to study at Eastney Modern for Boys, Portsmouth.

Bruce started his career at Portsmouth Dockyard as an apprentice Laboratory Mechanic. Four years later after leaving school without a single O level he had gained sufficient qualifications to go on to study at honours level an Electrical & Electronic Engineering Sandwich degree course at Portsmouth Polytechnic and from there worked in large scale manufacturing around the country, where his highlight was working in an aerospace & automotive factory in Paris with 1,200 French colleagues. Bruce's role at this facility was technical lead for the UK sales office for which learning to speak French was essential.

Years later his manufacturing career effectively came to an end when British American Tobacco closed its manufacturing facility in Southampton due to a change in government legislation. However by then Bruce had gained a merit pass in a Master of Business Administration postgraduate degree at the University of Southampton.

With his manufacturing career firmly behind him, Bruce turned his attention to politics, where in 2006 he became a Councillor at his first attempt in Eastleigh. By 2011, Bruce had gained another postgraduate degree, this time a merit pass in a Master of Public Administration at the University of Portsmouth and serves on four local councils including Hampshire County Council.

His most recent highlight was being invited by the Chinese government to give lectures on British democracy at several universities in southern China. He strongly supports education and manufacturing, and plans to use his influence to encourage Chinese students to study at British universities. To this end Bruce has enrolled on a Mandarin course at Southampton University.

Having lived in Fair Oak for 20 years I am saddened at how our community is being affected by thoughtless over development. Infrastructure is ignored, so schools, dentists, GP surgeries and roads are overwhelmed.

And this is before the One Horton Heath development (owned, designed and given permission by Eastleigh Borough Council) comes in.

For too long, Eastleigh has been ruled as a One Party State. It's time to call time on them.

My name is Ben Parry and I have lived with my family in Fair Oak for over 13 years. I believe it’s time you had a credible candidate that understands we can no longer afford to take a ‘one or the other’ approach on the economy and the environment; I firmly believe I am that credible candidate. The Covid pandemic, and the climate crisis, have shown us that governance at any level cannot continue to be ‘business as usual’. It has also emphasised the importance of a strong community. My children attend a junior and secondary school in the local area. Until recently, I was a teacher for 17 years; teaching in local secondary schools and in an Eastleigh sixth form college. Now, as a director of an Eastleigh-based traffic survey company, I see how heavy investment in local housing has not been matched by investment in proper infrastructure. My election would see me focus on:

Standing up for the economy • support local businesses to be successful and sustainable.

Standing up for the community • Help the community transition towards a carbon zero future. • Fight for a green transport infrastructure that alleviates the stress on our roads. 

Standing up for our green spaces • Protect the communal green spaces and ancient woodland that help protect our physical and mental health.

 Most importantly, I would be an independent voice on Eastleigh Borough Council, able to speak up with your best interests at heart, not the best interests of a party.

🙋‍♂️ Introducing Myself — Steve Humby, Reform UK Candidate for West End South

Hi everyone! I'm Steve Humby, and I'm standing as your Reform UK candidate for West End South on Eastleigh Borough Council and Hampshire County Council at the May 7th local elections.

I live in Hamble and have proudly called this corner of Hampshire home for 38 years. You might know me from my time serving the community — I spent 31 years as a Retained Firefighter and 8 years as a Wholetime Firefighter with Hampshire & IOW Fire and Rescue Service. I also served as a Special Constable based at Bitterne and Park Gate, and as an Ambulance Co-Responder. Public service has always been at the heart of who I am.

So what's brought me to politics? Honestly — frustration. A recent experience dealing with my Parish Council opened my eyes to just how painful and unnecessarily bureaucratic local government can be. If it felt that way to me, I can only imagine how it feels for other ordinary residents trying to get things done. That has to change.

If elected, my priorities will be:
✅ Tackling Eastleigh's growing council debt
✅ Helping ease the cost of living for local families
✅ Simply working for the people of West End — not for the system

I'm not a career politician. I'm a retired firefighter who has spent nearly four decades living and serving in this community. I'd love your support — and more than that, I'd love to hear what matters most to you.

👍 Follow my Facebook page to keep up with my campaign, and feel free to drop me a message anytime. Let's work together for West End — vote Steve Humby on May 7th!

#ReformUK #WestEndSouth #Eastleigh #Hampshire #StepUpForWestEnd #VoteSteveHumby

profile.statement_to_voters:
I have lived in Greater Manchester for the past three years, putting down roots after buying my first home locally. Working as a teacher in Manchester, I quickly became active in community life, helping to launch the Tameside LGBT Forum and previously serving as a trustee and later Chair of Salford Pride, the organisers of the annual Pink Picnic event. I led the charity through a difficult period of transition and renewal.

During the COVID-19 pandemic, I founded our local mutual aid group, coordinating support for thousands of vulnerable residents. A champion of community cohesion as someone whose family is of migrant descent, I believe strongly in equality, inclusion and the value everyone brings to our communities, particularly at a time when some seek to divide and scapegoat.

Liam is a Stalybridge resident and a proud husband and father of two.

An adult nurse with 9 years’ experience - including 4 years in cancer care at The Christie NHS Foundation Trust.  Liam brings real frontline insight into how our health services works under pressure.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Karen Rowland is an Abbey ward resident, passionate about achieving the best possible quality of life for everyone in Abbey. She is invigorated by our historic town-centre ward which is also at the epicentre of Reading's rapidly evolving future. However, Karen is determined that this progress does not come at the expense of the most vulnerable in our society, nor does it make Reading a town we cannot recognise in 10 years. She lays a large amount of blame directly at the feet of the current government for the sustained austerity that has negatively affected residents' public services and their quality of life.
Whilst completing her Masters degree in Historic Conservation, Karen has been a very engaged community activist, starting the Baker Street Area Neighbourhood Association(BSANA) in 2012 to resolve issues in her own area. Her natural eagerness to meet and engage with residents has helped to get to the roots of some very challenging issues and she has learned the right buttons to push in order to tackle them. She is a great believer in the power of communities to work with the Council in tackling issues; something that she hopes to encourage if elected. 
Additionally, Karen is a proud trustee of both the Reading Association for the Blind and the Reading Community Learning Centre. Both are organisations that work hard to maximise integration and opportunities for our large disabled and BAME communities. 
Karen is a pragmatist and prefers to keep her politics real, effective and focused. She knows that the Labour party's goals of a fairer life for all can be a reality. Moving to Reading as an EU immigrant in 2012, the way Brexit is managed in the years ahead is also of personal concern to her.  She is eager that residents contact her with their concerns.

Promoted by Chris Gavin on behalf of the Reading and District Labour Party, PO Box 8188, Reading, RG30 9LE

I was born right here in Reading Central, in the Royal Berkshire Hospital, and grew up in Spencers Wood.  Like you, I’m tired of feeling that Britain just doesn’t work any more.

After leaving to study Computer Science at university, I returned during the Covid-19 pandemic to train as a teacher at the University of Reading.  I now live here in central Reading, and proudly work for the local NHS in digital services, having previously worked as a civil servant and consultant.

I see first-hand, through my work in the NHS, the disastrous state the country is in - so I’m standing for Parliament to do something about it.  Fixing our NHS, our economy and our country won’t be easy, but with your help, we can get our town the fair deal we know it deserves.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Statement for WSCC Elections 2026
This election is for a Councillor to serve for the last two years of the County Council before it is replaced by a Unitary Authority in 2028. There will be separate elections for that council next year. Ensuring that there is a smooth transition into the new council, with no interruption to our local services, will be challenging.  As your current County Councillor, I am asking for your vote on the 7th May so that I can complete this important work.

If re-elected, I will continue to work for:
•	Proper repairs to potholes and resurfacing as an alternative to patching.
•	Better enforcement to stop fly-tipping, anti-social parking and speeding on rural    rat runs.
•	Reduced congestion at the Felbridge junction and along the A22.
•	Investment in the town centre and more effective use of the financial contributions from developers to provide new community buildings and sports facilities.
•	The relocation of Imberhorne Middle School to Imberhorne Lane
•	Timely completion of Special Educational Needs assessments and delivery of the support recommended
•	A strategic approach to new housing that respects our Neighbourhood Plans, delivers the necessary new infrastructure and protects our countryside and heritage buildings.

As an Independent Councillor I don’t have to follow a party line, I am free to decide every issue on its merits and to work with other parties to implement the policies and services which are in your best interests.

I grew up in Scarborough and came South to read Physics at the University of Kent.  I studied molecular liquids for my PhD using the experimental reactors at Harwell and the Institute Laue Langevin in Grenoble.  After completing my thesis I joined the MOD working on the development of new land and air systems at Fort Halstead and Farnborough.  My work included the development of combat simulations to support procurement decisions.  I became a Fellow of the Institute of Physics in 1999 and transferred to the DTI to lead a review of the Research Councils and Project Manage the successful £250M Diamond Synchrontron  bid to Treasury.  I attended the Royal College of Defence Studies  in 2003 and my final appointment was as Director for Space Technology and Industrial Policy in the forerunner of the UK Space Agency.

I initially became involved in local government to help prepare the Crawley Down Neighbourhood Plan. I was elected to Mid Sussex District Council in 2019 and re-elected in 2023.   I lead the Council's Independent Group and have been the Cabinet portfolio holder for Sustainable Economy and Housing in a joint Lib Dem/Independent administration for the past 3 years. I was also elected to West Sussex County Council in 2021 as an Independent for East Grinstead and Crawley Down. At WSCC, I sit on the Performance and Finance Scrutiny Committee and the Planning Committee.  I am also a member of East Grinstead Town Council so I am the elected representative for my residents across all three tiers of local government.  This means that my residents can bring any problem with local government to me.  The problems that my residents seek my help for most are the lack of doctors appointments, the quality of social housing,  delays in the processing of EHCPs, and insufficient school places.  I have seen the anxiety that these problems create and improving these services is a priority for me.

Born in the UK to South American parents, Andre spent many years overseas, and when he came back, East Grinstead became his home. For almost five years, he has lived in Imberdown with his wife and kids, building strong, genuine connections with the people and places here. He has spent these years working in education, teaching young people across secondary schools, which has deepened his commitment to supporting the community.

Professionally, he works as a Humanities Teacher. His experience includes:

• Secondary school teaching
• Working with SEND students
• Community involvement with Blackwell Primary School (supporting the PTA), Participation in East Grinstead Community and Leisure Centre, Volunteering with the local Scout, Regular attendance at town events

“My priority is to support our future generations and their environment, giving them hope in an achievable and realistic way, and strengthening their connections with our local communities, which are our pillars, especially in these uncertain times. I am committed to supporting our local schools by offering the help they need and acting as one of the intermediaries between them, governors, Mid Sussex Council, and West Sussex Council.

I am also focused on protecting our green spaces and promoting more community action to keep them safe, including much-loved areas like Worth Way. Improving opportunities for young people’s wellbeing is equally important to me, and I aim to work with local partners to increase access to leisure, sport, and safe environments. I also want to encourage more support and discussion sessions within our community to help improve young people’s mental and physical health.”

profile.statement_to_voters:
I'm proud to be standing for election on 7th May.  I'm hoping for your vote so I can start to get things done for our community.

I've lived in Suffolk for nearly 10 years - it's a beautiful county I've come to love.  I care deeply about its precious environments and habitats - I am a trustee of the Lavenham Woodland Project.  I also volunteer with the Sutton Hoo Ship Company and row with Woodbridge Coastal rowing Club.  I am widowed, with three grown-up children and five grandchildren.  I am a Quaker.

I have the skills for the job - and the track record to prove it.  Before retiring, I spent over 30 years in public service with the Health and Safety Executive, helping to make our lives safer and healthier.  That included working with local councils to make sure things got done.  I'm a graduate of Cambridge University, with a postgraduate degree in environmental planning.

Here are some of the things I want to get done:
- Fix our roads - as a motorist, I keep asking why are our roads so bad?
- Affordable housing - I remember the struggle to get on the housing ladder, and I want young people to have access to proper affordable homes.
- Rural buses - I have a bus pass, but where are the buses to go with it?
- Our waterways - as an angler and a rower, I want our rivers and streams properly cared for for future generations
- More woodland - as a custodian of a local wood, I know how beneficial it is for wildlife and for our wellbeing.

This is my first step into politics.  I'm standing because I believe we urgently need hope - not more despair and division.  We need a fresh start, and the Green Party can deliver that.

I am a full time mum and volunteer and I currently live in Winchester. I moved to Hampshire 10 years ago, after studying Sociology and Education and completing a PhD at Cardiff University. Jason was born in Bristol in the heart of a spirited, working-class community which he is immensely proud of. Now living in North Norfolk with his wife, Marina and their heavily doted on Labrador, Betty.

Currently a Mechanical Engineer in the oil and gas industry by trade, however, prior to this served in the Royal Navy for over 20 years as a Marine Engineer.

Jason first got involved in politics campaigning for UKIP and subsequently Reform UK, in local elections. 

Prime political beliefs:

Jason is passionate about our nation and wants a positive discussion about what we have achieved as opposed to the constant negativity of our failures.

Jason is an advocate for democracy which was his driver for leaving the European Union. Now, Jason will argue in favour of Proportional Representation as Britain’s political landscape is antiquated and requires reform. How can UKIP receive the confidence of almost 4 million voters in 2015 but only get one seat and yet, the SNP receive 1.5 million votes, get 56 seats? This simply doesn't make sense in the 21st century. 

He is a fervent campaigner for equality and raises concerns with Diversity, Equity and Inclusion (DEI) which he believes is creating divisions in society and that meritocracy must prevail at all times. Britain eliminated the divisions of employing people based on colour and gender years ago but now it seems, has reverted to that philosophy where he believes, will lead to a precarious situation in the future - equality for all under one law must be our focus.

Defence of the realm – our numbers are low in all services to which, the reinvestment of our nations defences is required. The world is going through tumultuous times and Britain is not prepared.

Jason is a good speaker and is keen on standing up in front of the public, highlighting Reform UK’s policies and indeed, entering articulate and levelled debates.

With North Norfolk being declared a dental desert, Jason is keen on ensuring that all its citizens, have easy and affordable dental care available and to eliminate the need for practitioners to leave the area. Jason would launch a campaign to help attract more dentists to our areas. As North Norfolk’s representative, Jason would engage and collaborate with the array of industries locally such as energy, farming and the manufacturing sectors which could help and play a part in the growth of the constituency.

A requirement for more GP's or surgeries, dentists, school places, more police, infrastructure is needed to which, Jason would make the case for in parliament.

Jason loves North Norfolk, its coastline, wildlife, landscapes and its communities. Let’s all shout out about our beautiful district and indeed country and highlight to the world what we have achieved and where we are going.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Hale has been taken for granted by the Greens for years. Whether it’s the appalling state of our roads, our blocked drains flooding streets, or our Green Councillor who has now become an MP in Manchester. Hale deserves better.

Since being elected, Cllr Natalie Shalom has worked tirelessly for residents, dealing with more than 100 individual casework enquiries since 20th November 2025 – getting potholes filled and drains unblocked.

But there is so much more to be done. Natalie can’t do it all on her own. She needs a strong team working alongside her.

You can help build a strong team in Hale by voting for me to work shoulder to shoulder with Natalie to get things done.

Your Conservative team has a strong record of being there for residents, including opposing plans to raise council tax by 7.5%, being the only party to vote against a 40% pay rise for councillors, opposing Labour’s £50 Green Bin Tax, forcing a public meeting on the Cresta Court and Ashley Hotels proposals while raising legitimate safety and community concerns, and campaigning for the basics residents expect: better roads, cleared drains, a new local secondary school, and improved local SEND provision.

Please ensure we have a strong local team standing up for Hale, making sure we get the roads fixed, drains cleared, and fight for our parks.

Rupert Kelly
Conservative Candidate for Hale

I'm Orla, your local Green Party candidate for Hale. As a resident living in the Hale ward, I know the difference that committed Green councillors make for the local community.

By keeping the Green team in Hale together, I can continue this hard work, and be a strong voice for positive change and inclusion in Hale, speaking up for all residents.

profile.statement_to_voters:
I’m Tessa, a local mum, a worker in the not-for-profit sector and your Green candidate in the May local council elections. I grew up in Ecclesall and now want to give back to this vibrant community.

**I can win and help bring hope back to Sheffield**

Across the country, more people are responding to the Green Party’s message of bringing hope back into politics. Here in Ecclesall, residents tell me they want a councillor who listens, who understands family life, and who will stand up for the area.

As a granddaughter, I supported my grandparents through Vascular Dementia. As a daughter, I lost my Mum when I was 24 and dealt with grief. As a mum, I’ve campaigned for cleaner air, more green spaces, play spaces for children and safer streets. I feel a deep compassion for people of all ages through my lived experience. I want Ecclesall to be a place where every family feels safe, supported, and glad to call home.

**Working for you all year round**

If elected, I would be proud to work alongside Green councillor Peter Gilbert, who won the last local elections in Ecclesall Ward. We will speak up for residents in council meetings, challenge decisions that don’t reflect our community’s needs, and work hard to deliver positive, practical change. Peter and I are involved with local community groups, speak with local businesses, and knock on doors to listen and support.

**Your vote counts on 7th May.**

Ecclesall deserves a future shaped by the people who live here and love this area. On 7th May, please use your vote to elect me as your representative on Sheffield City Council — so together, we can build a cleaner, safer, more hopeful future.

prior_electoral_record.organisation: Borough Council of King's Lynn and West Norfolk

profile.statement_to_voters:
I am concerned at the scale of the climate and ecological crisis that we are facing, and the wide-spread corruption and failure of our public institutions in performing their basic duties - and the almost complete lack of understanding of these issues among all the political parties, including sadly, the Green Party of which I used to be a member. I was elected me as a district councillor in May 2023 and in this role, among other things, I have successfully stoped a mega-poultry and pig farm from coming to West Norfolk, fought for and obtained guarantees of a competition-grade swimming pool to replace the existing outdated pool, helped several residents obtain warm home heating grants and got a garden wildlife competition underway to encourage people to look after the wildlife in their backyards. I'm a GP by profession, and this involves regularly talking to hundreds of people from all walks of life. I believe this breadth of experience has stood me in good stead in the rough and tumble world of politics and media coverage. I write a monthly Politics opinion column for the Lynn News which has been generally well received. My contributions as vice-chair of the Norfolk health overview scrutiny committee have been praised by journalists as well as colleagues from other parties. 
If elected I will be a champion for Downham Market which has great potential to become a beacon for a sustainable, healthy, and joyful way of life.

I will be standing as the Labour candidate for the Downham division of Norfolk County Council on the 7th May.

This is an election for a soon to be abolished council, but important decisions will be made about the future of local government for decades. After three years of representing the place I live at Borough, I am ready to help shape that future, ensuring great services are in place for residents.

Terry Jermy MP and the Labour Government has been delivering for Downham, as we saw with the confirmation of the SEND school at the Ryston End site. We need Norfolk County Council fully focused on delivering the best possible services for residents.

The Tories lost Downham Old Town to me in 2023.
The Tories lost SW Norfolk to Labour in 2024.
The Tories will likely be losing County in 2026.

The Reform Party offer the Tories' failed policies repackaged with a lot more divisive rhetoric that I do not think Norfolk will benefit from. Focusing on who we are against should not be our priority, but instead working together to build a better Norfolk and a fairer society.

Too many live in hardship or poverty, every visit to a shop stressful, and events half a world away will continue to impact on our cost of living. We and our area's precious wildlife are also threatened by the rapidly changing climate, and we must consider and adapt to swiftly evolving technologies.

Yet there is a lot to hope for in Downham Market, with our thriving high street, independent businesses, and many successful charities and organisations tackling some of those issues directly.

Together, we achieve more than we do alone, and that is why I am standing for election on the 7th May.

Although James grew up in Shepherd’s Bush, he has proudly called Broxbourne home since 2010. He has built his career in senior partnership and strategy roles across the telecoms and retail sectors, bringing a practical, results-driven approach to everything he does.

While studying Environmental Economics as part of his Master’s degree, James developed a strong belief that a thriving economy and a greener future go hand in hand. He understands that sustainable growth isn’t a trade-off — it’s an opportunity.

Locally, James is passionate about preserving Broxbourne’s open spaces and improving public transport, making the area an even better place to live, work, and raise a family.

There’s a saying: “You are a product of your environment.” James believes this applies at every level. By improving the environment in Broxbourne and beyond, we can create stronger communities and a better quality of life for everyone.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Born and bred here in Oxford, raising two young children, and fighting for our community every day.

I’ve served as a City Councillor for over 7  years and as Cabinet Member for a Safer Oxford for over 2 years, tackling the issues that matter most to local families.

I’ve delivered safer streets, pushed for better housing and green spaces, and fought crime and anti-social behaviour alongside Thames Valley Police.

Blackbird Leys deserves respect, not to be left behind.

If you want a councillor who shows up, stands up and gets things done, I’m asking for your vote on 7th May.

Blackbird Leys first. Every street matters. Every family counts.

Keep in touch

In Blackbird Leys, families are doing everything right working, raising children, contributing to our community. But too many are still falling behind by rising bills, rising rents and services stretched.

Please get in touch if there’s anything I can help with: lubna.labour@gmail.com.

Indigo Haynes (she/her) is a modern languages student in her final year at Oxford University, with plans to start a teacher training course in Oxford after graduating.

She's been involved with local politics since she was a teenager, having run campaigns in her hometown to save a local hospital from shutdown, get better bus routes with cheaper fares, and led climate change protests and 'Fridays For Future' school walk-outs.

She was also a member of Youth Parliament, and secretary of her local Labour Party; since Starmer's election as Prime Minister, she left the party, feeling betrayed by his government's actions. Now in Oxford, her focus is on the same issues she's always fought for: preserving local services, improving public transport, and working to fix the climate crisis.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Since first being elected as a Councillor in 2019, I have developed a passion for our District. I have worked tirelessly to represent our residents, businesses and stakeholders at a local and National level.

Most notably, I became leader of the Council in May, the first women and youngest ever leader of RDC, and instantly took the fight to Government to challenge their ridiculous housing targets. I am prepared to defend our Green Belt with everything I have. I have been consistent with my views and my approach.

But being a Cllr isn’t just about the big things, it’s arguably just as important dealing with the case work, and helping residents with the things that matter most, be it a missed bin collection, or a major housing issue, I have proven consistently I will help, even when it’s not in my remit to do so.

I have diarised my journey on Facebook through the years, so that you can see I woke all year round, year in, year out - not just when there is an election.

I take the party out of politics and just work for the residents I represent, and have done so this year in collaboration with two Independant groups, for the good our district.

My name is John-Barry Waldron and I'm excited to be running as a candidate for the green party in the upcoming local elections. I am passionate about preserving the local environment and green spaces. Working with the local community to advocate for them on local issues. 

As a mental health nurse, I have witnessed the impact that cuts to local services have had on the mental health of individuals and the community. That’s why I am committed to making mental health a priority in this local election, including ensuring that everyone has better access to local services and resources. 

I believe that the environment, local services and mental health are interconnected and that by prioritising them, we can create a stronger, more resilient community that is able to face the challenges of the future. 

As a candidate for the green party, I am committed to working hard on behalf of the community and strongly supporting policies that will support the well-being of all of us. I am excited to work with you and to make a positive difference in the community.

Thank you for considering my candidacy and I look forward to earning your support in this election.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Birmingham is at a crossroads after 14 years of Labour misrule which resulted in huge council tax rises, devastation of public services and finally - bankruptcy. Only the Local Conservatives have the experienced leadership to start to put things right on Day One after the election and have a costed and detailed six point plan to put the city back on the right road. This plan includes fixing the roads quickly to prevent more expensive repairs later, ending the bin strike and continuing weekly collections, protecting the city’s green spaces, cleaning up the streets and dealing with badly run HMOs and balancing the budget. Only voting for the Conservatives can guarantee that we will not be landed with another desolate 4 years of Labour control.

I have had my home in the Northfield area all my life. It really is home to me. I know the people and what’s happening here. If elected I will of course stand up for Northfield and carry out a weekly ward round to keep me in touch with the people and the area in which we live. If something’s not right I will do my best to get it put right. And I will support the Conservative administration to help it set the city back on its feet.

Birmingham needs a Conservative administration to undo all the damage done by Labour and Northfield can do its part by voting for me on 7 May to ensure a Local Conservative councillor is playing his part to help get all the necessary work done as soon as possible.

I’m Esther Rai, your Labour candidate for the Northfield ward by-election on Thursday 4 July 2024.

I’ve spent my working career as a teacher and in community support work, advocating for children, young people, and the most vulnerable.

As your councillor I will stand up for your interests, be present in the community, and champion Northfield.

Let’s get Northfield back on track.

My pledges to you: 

1. Be present within our community - I will get the basics right - I’ll hold regular ward forums and advice surgeries and get our ward plan over the line.

2. Fight for every penny we’re entitled to - I’ll make sure all of our ward funds are spent on improving Northfield, and that we get a fair share of new pots of money.

3. Work towards a better future for our high street - I’ll work with the BID and Council to make sure we’re ready to win future funding and to stop anti-social parking from food couriers.

4. Stand up for you during the Council’s recovery - I’ll be your voice in the room to make sure decisions are made with the people of Northfield, including saving Northfield Library.

5. Make our streets safe and accessible - I’ll work with authorities to improve walking, wheeling and bus routes, and with the PCC to reduce crime on our streets.

I am 24 years old and work as a plumber, running a small business serving customers across Birmingham, my working life is grounded in the real economy. Working in people’s homes, I speak to ordinary residents every day and see first-hand the challenges families face with local services and the condition of our city.

My interest in politics began in my early teens, when I started to reject the prevailing left-wing worldview that had been presented to me for much of my life.

Even at that age, I felt strongly that it was traditional values that hold our society together family, community, faith, and national identity and that these were increasingly being undermined in the name of “progress.”

Around that same time, I also found my Christian faith, something I hope continues to shape my understanding of responsibility and service to others.

This election feels different because the political landscape itself is changing.

We are witnessing the growth of sectarian politics and the "unholy" coalition on the left.

I feel it is essential that residents are offered a credible alternative, one rooted in British values, civic responsibility, and respect for our nation.

That’s why I’m standing for Reform UK.

We are the only alternative. Reform is the only party willing to challenge the political consensus that has dominated British politics for too long.

I am excited to be part of this once in a lifetime political revolution, and if elected will work tirelessly to deliver for residents on that basis.

profile.statement_to_voters: Kay Mitchell has lived in Southend for more than 30 years and brought up her family here. Having worked across support services most of her working life Kay has a clear understanding of social and emotional health, as well as systems of support. She has experience of multi agency working and managing case work. As a former councillor and cabinet member she is familiar with council processes and procedures. Kay is an activist for the NHS and preserving resident support services, having seen the impact of cuts over many years across the city. Kay has been involved in projects with residents including getting road and footway repairs done, pushing for Chalkwell access ramp to be reinstated, and attending water quality meetings. Kay regularly takes residents concerns on health, investment in infrastructure, transport and regreening the city to council meetings, while standing up for staff and students at the university and supporting protection of our green belt. Kay is a co-founder of a family friendly multi cultural festival celebrating our performers, art, culture and community, that also promotes local business. Kay has demonstrated that she is  able to represent residents as a Green Party candidate working directly with residents on their priorities and continuing to improve outcomes in Chalkwell, applying strong values in social and environmental justice, and a good quality of life for all.

Chalkwell is one of Southend's jewels in the crown with our beautiful beachfront, excellent local park and friendly neighbourhoods.

I bring with me vast experience as a procurement professional of 19 years, spanning the public and charity sectors. I started my career at Southend City (then Borough) Council before working within the NHS and then the City of London Corporation. I currently work for a research charity in London supporting cutting-edge scientific discovery. My career has been dedicated to serving the public interest, which goes to the core of why I have decided to stand.

Through my work, I have witnessed first-hand the importance of local democracy, efficient public services, and robust support systems for vulnerable members of our community.

Furthermore, my experience in procurement has instilled in me a strong sense of integrity, business ethics and a drive to ensure value for money. I understand the importance of allocating resources wisely and making data-based decisions. I will work diligently to ensure that our Council’s budget is utilised effectively and to address the needs of our community.

Above all, I am deeply invested in the future of our community and am dedicated to improving the outcomes for all residents from East to West, North to South.

With your support, I am eager to roll up my sleeves and work tirelessly to build a brighter future for all of our families and generations to come.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Whoever is leading the Government on June 9th, the Isle of Wight will not be their top priority, however as the Island’s MP it will be my single point of focus. Many people feel it is time we had someone representing them that will be solely focused on representing the Island because successive Governments have failed to understand the unique challenges we face. We know we have the ability to progress with a hand up, not just handouts. I believe we should be looking for an Island deal, an opportunity for real localism, to enable effective local governance and a chance to steer ourselves to a place of wellbeing and prosperity.

WELLBEING & PROSPERITY, Three Principles:

1. Economic -Building a strong, resilient and competitive economy
2. Social - supporting strong, vibrant and healthy communities
3. Environment – Protecting and enhancing our natural, built and historic environment

Economy:
• Small business micro funding and improved support.
• Cut VAT for Tourism.
• Circular Economy and the Island pound.
Transport:
• Back the feasibility study to determine facts regarding any fixed link.
• Work on solutions for rural bus travel.
• Create infrastructure for pedestrians and cyclists for able-bodied and disabled users.
• Road safety -20mph in residential streets and town and village centres.
Education:
• Make wellbeing as important as learning.
• Rebalance academic and vocational studies.
• Adjust the school terms for improved family life and educational attainment.
• Early Special Educational Needs diagnosis with ongoing support.

Health:
• Support the team at St Mary’s Hospital for improving and retaining services.
• Increase mental health provision for early intervention, especially for anxiety.
• Adult Social Care, lobby to end the post code lottery of care.
• More care for carers, so they can continue to support their loved ones.
• Raise aspirations for young people to choose a career in health

Housing:
• Build affordable rental properties in sustainable brown-field sites.
• Support housing that can be built quickly using pre-fabricated methods.
• Counter the negative effects that land banking can have on a community.

Environment
• Campaign for a smart grid for solar energy
• Wind turbines in industrial parks, the Island a beacon for green energy
• Protect our AONB status

More at: https://www.juliejonesevans.org/commitments

profile.statement_to_voters:
Lifelong Tamworth resident and builder, I’ve stood twice for election in Tamworth and I’m standing again because I believe I can make a difference by listening to you and using my real-world experience to deliver the kind of Tamworth that you want to see.
Local Government is changing and I want to be there to oversee a smooth transition, ensuring democracy at the local level is maintained, your voice is heard and our community heritage is preserved.
Labour and Conservative parties alike have consistently failed us, do you think the £21.6million “Levelling Up” funds were well spent in the town on some wonky new paving, some poky little shop units and a new college that was full to capacity on the day that it opened? Were you asked for your views on where this money should have been spent?
What we need is an environment that is well maintained, fostering pride in our community. Public transport that actually turns up when it is supposed to. Community banking hubs to replace the banks that are abandoning us. Public spaces where we don’t just survive, but thrive.
People want change, but don’t look in the direction of Reform to deliver it, since taking control of the County Council in May 2025 the only change they seem to have delivered is their leader (3 in the space of 4 months) every time a racist social media post comes to light. Theirs is the politics of Division, Isolation and Hate. Ours is of Inclusion, Community and Hope.
May 7th can be the turning point where we collectively send the message that we want something better not just for our future but for that of our children and generations to come.

I've lived near Woodley for over 20 years. I worked as an accountant and for local charities, before I recently retired. Now I volunteer on the till of the Oxfam bookshop in Woodley on Saturday mornings, am a churchwarden at Earley St Peter's and have been a member of Earley Town Council for the last 3 years.

I'm interested in the environment, transport, fair trade, and the eradication of poverty locally, nationally and around the world. I joined the Liberal Democrats because I feel the two main parties are out of touch with local issues and don't care about local people and their concerns. I also realised that just voting in elections didn't change much so I decided to get involved in local politics and try to make a difference.

I have been a proud resident of Wokingham Borough since 2005. My children have grown up here, attended local schools, and enjoyed our beautiful parks. Through door-to-door conversations, I have developed a deep understanding of the issues that matter most to the residents of Bulmershe and Coronation ward.

1. Parking Fee Adjustments: o   Advocate for the repeal of recent increments in parking fees. o   Reinstate complimentary parking on Sundays and evenings to invigorate local economic vitality and bolster businesses.

2. Sanitation Management: o   Uphold weekly refuse collection schedules to ensure communal satisfaction and ecological purity. o   Prevent fly-tipping, which exacts considerable financial and environmental tolls.

3. Primary and Secondary School Education o   Number of school places need to be increased. o   Work thoroughly to increase extra-curricular and sports activities for young people.

 4. Community Safety Enhancement: o   Intensify collaborations with law enforcement to efficaciously address anti-social behaviour and reduce incidences of shoplifting. o   Implement regulatory measures for electric scooters to safeguard pedestrian safety.

6. Sustainable Development and Environmental Stewardship: o   Commit to the conservation of green areas to strike an equilibrium between urban development and ecological preservation. o   Acknowledge the enduring advantages of sustainable land management on property valuations and communal well-being.

I hold a Bachelor of Engineering in Electronics and have completed the Oxford University Executive Leadership Programme. Currently, I serve as a Human Resources Director in the telecommunications sector and am a member of the PBBC Business Council, where I support local businesses and promote bilateral trade. I am a keen golfer, love planting trees, and am a nature loving enthusiast.

Your vote is powerful. With your support on May 2nd, I promise to be a vigorous advocate for our community, working tirelessly to make Wokingham Borough an even better place to live, learn, work, and raise a family.

I am proud to be standing as the Conservative candidate to represent Blunsdon, Highworth and our local villages following the retirement of popular Councillor Alan Bishop.

Having served as a Highworth Town Councillor for 8 years, Highworth Mayor in 201 7, a school governor and various local charity and community roles, I have shown I have the experience to make a difference.

As a family we have been heavily involved with supporting the Highworth market, volunteering at the annual May Day and Christmas Lights events, various groups for young people, community litter pics and The Link Magazine. I am a strong voice against inappropriate development and I am an active member of the 'Save The Golf Course Group.'

I also understand the importance of protecting the character of our wonderful villages: Blunsdon, Castle Eaton, Hampton, Hannington, Hannington Wick, Inglesham, Sevenhampton & Stanton Fitzwarren. I will also work with the respective local Town & Parish Councils, press for the final areas to get superfast fibre broadband, support the amazing Blunsdon Community Shop, the Highworth High Street and all the local village pubs.

I would be very proud to represent our community alongside Justin Tomlinson MP, Cllrs Vijay Manro & Steve Weisinger - a strong team. I very much hope you will use your postal vote to vote for me - I will be a hardworking, visible and approachable Councillor.

Rural communities are too often overlooked and ignored when decisions are made. For the past 13 years, I’ve worked on the railway, and alongside that I served for 12 years as a Special Constable in the police. Like many residents, I want to see sensible planning that protects our green spaces, and a council that listens to all residents, including those in villages and rural areas.

I would be honoured to have your support.

Hossain Tafazzal is married with two children and four grandchildren, and works at Domino's Pizza in Rugby. Hossain moved to Rugby in 2010, and for the past four years has lived in Garyth Williams Close.

He is Vice President of the Coventry Bangladesh National Party, a former Secretary of the Bangladesh Cultural Association in Rugby, and is still a member of the Association's governing committee.

Hossain has a desire to be a councillor in Rugby so that he can work for local residents and communities and strengthen the Liberal Democrat group's voice on the council.

Hossain joined the Liberal Democrats because they strongly support Britain's membership of the European Union and they believe in improving educational opportunities; they want better access to primary health care such as GP surgeries; they oppose racism; they believe in protecting and encouraging small businesses.

Hossain first worked with the Liberal Democrats when assisting Phil Bennion in his attempt to get re-elected as a Member of the European Parliament for the West Midlands in 2015. In particular, he has assisted Carie-Anne Dumbleton, Bill Lewis and Glenda Allanach in the work they do in their Ward. question: Who will win the Wigan West ward in the Wigan local election on 2026-05-07?

profile.statement_to_voters:
LIZ HARSANT – A STRONG VOICE FOR RESIDENTS IN HOLYWELLS
Liz Harsant has been a resident of Ipswich for many years and a Councilor for 17. In this time, she has been leader of Ipswich Borough Council and worked timelessly to empower residents to improve their neighbourhoods and lives.
She has been instrumental in the Waterfront Regeneration project; The Suffolk University build and the renovation of Holywells Park. Most recently working with the Rivers Action Group to represent residents opposed to the Upper Orwell Crossing which was retracted.
Liz has secured funding to help refurbish the facilities of Patrick’s Boxing Club in York Road, a club that helps young adults to spend time getting fit, active and off the streets. Liz would like Ipswich Borough Council to recognise Patrick’s as a great community asset and other facilities in Ipswich that also serve the community. ‘More facilities like Patrick’s can be the lifeblood for younger adults and help reduce antisocial behaviour. We need more funding from our Borough to provide these vital services,’ explains Liz.
Liz is a compassionate, dedicated Councilor and if re-elected she will be working to: • Tackle antisocial behavior. • Combat parking and traffic congestion especially due to the closing of the Orwell Bridge in extreme winds. • Improve mental health services by working to reduce waiting times for residents, so they can access support services quickly.
“Improving services and facilities will combat antisocial behavior and improve the neighborhoods we all live in. I want to empower residents and communities to live well and be safe in our town,’ said Liz.
YOUR PRIORITIES ARE MY PRIORITIES
RESIDENTS FIRST, PARTY SECOND 
Liz has always put the interests of her residents first, even when it has clashed with her party's views. She worked with the Rivers Action Group representing residents opposed to the Upper Orwell Crossing, which helped result in the scheme's cancellation.
BOXING CLEVER! 
Liz has worked with Patrick’s Boxing Club in York Road which helps and encourages young people to take up boxing as a sport. The gym was badly in need of renovation and Liz managed to obtain funding for this. Schemes like this can provide opportunities for young people and help reduce anti-social behaviour. If re-elected, Liz will fight for more to be done for young people in the area.
LIZ GETS THINGS DONE
Liz has established two defibrillators in Holywells Ward, one in Duke Street and one at the Margaret Catchpole Bowls Club (pictured). She also opened the Baby Bereavement Garden which she was instrumental in helping with. As your Councillor for 17 years, Liz is well known as someone who gets things done.

Dr Gurpreet Padda (she/her) has today 20th March 2024 been selected as parliamentary candidate for Waveney Valley.



As an environmental scientist, Gurpreet is committed to working hard in the area of environmental science and conservation. She has worked at the Centre for Environment Fisheries and Aquaculture Science (Cefas) lab in Lowestoft and is working in government, and completed her PhD in Environmental Sciences at the University of East Anglia. Gurpreet is founder of Black Ocean Citizens, a non-profit aimed at connecting diverse and underrepresented communities to water-based and ocean sciences. As well as working in Norwich, she also serves Lakenham ward as Norwich City Councillor and is involved in decisions concerning the provision of affordable housing, tackling crime, equality & diversity, climate change and creating opportunities for local training and employment.



With ties across Waveney Valley, Norfolk and Suffolk, Gurpreet lives in Norwich with her husband, two children and Luna the dog. Daughter of first generation immigrants, Gurpreet understands the value of hard work and being part of a community. She enjoys listening to music, spending time with her family and friends outdoors and on the river, fishing.



Gurpreet says "I am aware of challenges facing the Waveney Valley community including poor services as a result of dwindling public finance and not having enough low carbon affordable homes to support growing local families. With Labour marked as the succeeding party, no other candidate can and will ensure local voices are heard in Parliament."



"I am excited to be representing Waveney Valley. With Labour in government, I want to be a strong advocate for local people in Westminster. Issues that are important to me are:



Cleaner -- As part of Labour's Clean Energy goals, hold water companies to account to ensure Good Environmental Status is achieved regarding the health of River Waveney

Greener -- With Labour's Green Prosperity and Great British Energy plans, mandate for low carbon affordable new homes to support local jobs and services



Fairer -- As part of Labour's modern childcare system, support local families with child care to enable families to stay local with better paid, good quality jobs, and low carbon affordable new homes."

I’m Richard “Dicky” Eminson. Im a semi retired airline steward, that worked on the airlines for eleven years. Born in Southport, on a horticultural farm. I had a strong work ethic drilled into me from around 8 years old, as I helped my father work the land, producing seasonal crops.

After obtaining a business degree at Lancs uni. I moved directly to London. After various jobs, which included 5 years as a floor manager in Harrods. I eventually settled in the East End of London with my partner. After a friend of mine, who was cabin crew, found that easyJet was recruiting. I found my dream job! I am the ultimate people person that thrive on hospitality.

Activities outside these work scenarios, I have volunteered on a befriending course for Crisis (the homeless charity). I have raised over £10,000 for The Anthony Nolan Trust, via two London marathons. As well as many fun runs for Cancer charities.

After living in Brighton for 7 years and running my own business. I came to Norfolk for a short break over a year ago and simply fell in love with the people  and relaxed vide. I reside in Central Norwich, so very accessible. I class myself as likeable and approachable.  And more importantly I try and be as supportive as I can to any community I reside.

As a council tenant here in Lakenham, I see what you see every day, I know it’s time for a change. 
I’m running to get the basics right: cleaning up our streets, ending fly-tipping, and ensuring our children have safe, clean places to play. I understand the frustration of waiting for the system to work, and I will be the voice that finally demands Lakenham’s fair share.

​For too long, we’ve been represented by people who don't live our reality. I am committed to fighting for the high-quality social housing and reliable services we deserve. 
If elected I will to take our shared experience to the Council to ensure no resident feels disenfranchised or ignored again.

1. **​Practical Politics** : I believe in common-sense solutions that make our lives easier—better maintenance, warmer homes, and cleaner streets.

​ 2.**Working for You**: I’m ready to put the hard graft in. You deserve a councillor who is visible, reachable, and understands the value of a pound.

​ 3.**A Green Future for All**: Going Green shouldn't be a luxury for the wealthy. It should mean lower bills and better living standards for everyone in our community.

​Lakenham doesn't need more empty promises. It needs a councillor who knows the reality of life in social housing and is willing to work for it.

question: Who will win the London Colney ward in the St Albans local election on 2026-05-07?

profile.statement_to_voters:
I’ve lived in the ward since 2012, when my wife and I got tired of the Parisian suburb, and decided to move to the UK. We have never regretted this decision, and thoroughly enjoyed living here with our 3 daughters. The youngest is currently at the Bulmershe School.

I’m a software engineer, and work at the ECMWF (European Center for Mid-range Weather Forecast, on Shinfield road). I commute with my bicycle of course: we've lived without a car for 5 years now. I'm part of Kidical Mass Reading, who organises family-friendly bike rides in town, to increase families’ confidence in cycling together, and to highlight the need for better cycling infrastructure.

If elected, I will campaign to reduce the place of cars in the public space, and promote the use of walking, cycling or public transports. Cars are necessary, but the fewer there are, the happier we get! I will also protect and improve the green space, and support our amazing local, independent businesses.  We all have a role to play to fight climate change.  At our level, this is also simply to improve the life of people in the borough!

I have lived in the local area with my family since 1988, so I am fully aware of the concerns that impact local residents because your issues are my issues.

I have been self-employed for the past 38 years and am committed to the growth and vitality of our local community.

At the heart of this is my belief that we must enhance the skills of young people and create opportunities for them to be responsive to economic, social, technological, and political changes. The recent pandemic has taught us all to reconsider the way we live, work and travel.

My experience as a borough councillor serving neighbouring Park ward in Reading, from 2007 – 2011, gave me valuable awareness of environment protection, local infrastructure, supporting local businesses, improving education, and tackling anti-social behaviour.

Public service is what drives me. I am committed to helping others and standing up for the residents of Bulmershe and Whitegates.

I can only do this with your support and trust. Please contact me to share your views or if you need any help.

I'm currently a Councillor in Maiden Erlegh and Whitegates. I was a District Councillor in Hong Kong too, where I worked hard to serve the community and strive for democracy. In 2021 I was forced to resign and leave Hong Kong for safety as Beijing carried out mass arrests of Democratic politicians. I now live in Earley with my family and have a son who attends Maiden Erlegh School.

To serve the community and to strive for fairness have been my aspiration since I was a kid. I enjoy solving people’s problems, making people’s voices heard, improving governance, upholding democratic values, and I volunteer in British Hong Kong people's organisations. I know how important a Councillor is to the local community and how symbolic it is to be the first former Hong Kong District Councillor elected in the UK. Although my mission was suppressed in Hong Kong, I'm honoured to serve the people of Maiden Erlegh and Whitegates and I do hope you will re-elect me in May.

question: Who will win the Bury West ward in the Bury local election on 2026-05-07?

profile.statement_to_voters:
As a Conservative, I am championing the Tunbridge Wells First campaign.

We face the most important point for Tunbridge Wells for many years. The Borough Council, will soon be abolished and a new West Kent or ‘all of’ Kent’ Unitary Authority created. I am campaigning for all discretionary services, local assets and appropriate services are assigned to a new Royal Tunbridge Wells Town Council. The Borough Council, with its existing Leaders, is more concerned with their position the West Kent. In other Boroughs, councils are signing letters of intent to allocate these assets, revenue and local services to their main towns. Our Council has been negative, claiming only allotments are guaranteed for inclusion by a new RTW town council.

As Dep. Chairman of the (non-political) RTW Town Forum, I did the detailed work (later confirmed by the Council) that showed we only receive £1.4 million is services specific to out town while RTW residents pay £2.8m in extra tax which should be earmarked for our town. This includes payments for our parks, the Common, community space and other discretionary services.

The strong finances and low council taxes of the Borough of Tunbridge Wells is based on the many assets accumulated over the last 30 years or primarily Conservative councils. Without the Borough’s ownership on the car parks, the RVP shopping plaza and many other assets, our taxes would be much higher. We would also not had the opportunity to buy the RVP lease without the foresight of Conservative Councils. We stand to loose so much if we allow all of these to be absorbed into the new Unitary Authority. They should be retained by a new Town Council.

I my tenure as Deputy Leader of the Borough Council during COVID, we helped ensure we had a positive accounting financial result throughout. I also set up and led the cross party investigation into the Town Hall. This committed, and achieved a unanimous agreement to transition it to shared commercial office and work space. I worked with developers, bus and taxis companies to investigate ways of financing town centre transport - a concept not followed by the current Lib Dem  Leadership. I championed the seating areas in the High Street and supporting an entrepreneur to clean up the Challybeate spring - making it an attracting feature while saving the council precious funds. I helped investigate and encourage the redevelopment of the old cinema site, meeting and problem solving with the previous owners and the new owners - Retirement Homes (a subsidiary of AXA)

Now the council plans to build a new cinema attached to RVP, borrowing £68m. At current interest rates (4.8% pa) now 5 times higher than pre-COVID rates (0.9%), the annual costs will be 5 times higher than the previous project. It is therefore critical a full economic case is made public, otherwise we could and up with a non-financial project draining council funds for years. Understand the economics is so important rather than simply the headline numbers and exaggerated cost numbers.

Tunbridge Wells parks and other assets should be owned and controlled locally.

I am standing for election as the Alliance candidate in Park so that I can continue to work hard for residents as a local, independent councillor, free from the influence of a national political party.

Why vote for me?

- I was part of the team that delivered a community funded playground in Calverley Grounds.

- I challenged the proposed development on the edge of Calverley Grounds for a new town hall, office block, underground car park and theatre. After several years, this project was cancelled. The development would have cost taxpayers at least £108 million, and was likely to exceed £150 million.

- I pushed for the unused space in the Town Hall to be rented out from 2017. The Town Hall is now a thriving building with many small businesses using the building alongside Tunbridge Wells Borough Council.

- I worked hard to save several bus routes serving Hawkenbury and Tunbridge Wells.

- My wife and I launched and organised the annual Town Wide Yard sale since 2017.

- I have helped residents pull together to challenge rogue builders.

- Now, I challenge the spending of £68 million to redevelop part of Royal Victoria Place. The cost is likely to rise above £100 million. There are much lower cost and effective plans that could bring life back into Ely Court and the old food court in the shopping centre.

- I will push for Royal Tunbridge Wells to get a Town Council and work to ensure it gets more than the default set of assets (the allotments) in the local government restructuring.

question: Who will win the Park ward in the Tunbridge Wells local election on 2026-05-07?

profile.statement_to_voters:
We need councils and councillors who are truly accountable to the electorate.

And we need a new kind of politics based on our national interest and on a wide and continuing public debate about what our national interest is. This way we can get government that actually represents us.

The public are fed up with tick-box consultation exercises where governments at the local and national level pretend to listen to ordinary people. And it's not good enough for politicians simply to tell us that "the science says" this or "the markets say" that so there is no alternative. We need a real political dialogue where new ideas can be contested and debated. This has to start at the local level.

In local elections, residents are concerned about potholes, waste collection, green spaces and our streets. In all of these areas, I will work to ensure that the council works with local people to support ground-up responses and actions so that residents can have real ownership of their community life.

Councillors often claim they cannot control the biggest part of their budget as it's spent on statutory services mandated by central Government. But councils look to outsource all this service provision to private companies, often owned abroad by venture capital. Why should our rising council taxes go to line the pockets of shareholders? I will campaign for more services to be provided in-house so that more of our taxes go to the local economy and councils are forced to be truly accountable for their budgets. This will not be done overnight. But even small steps will increase the knowledge available in-house, meaning that councils will be better able to negotiate with external providers and local government will build up its capacity to provide services.

Vote for me in Sale Central if you want a councillor who will work to close the gap between local government and the people it is supposed to serve.

I'm standing as a candidate in Sale Central because I want to represent young people and give them a seat at the table. I have deep concerns about climate change and I am proud to stand for social justice. It would be an honour to be the first Green Party councillor in Sale Central :)

We live in a Diverse community  which gives us opportunity to value each other and respect each other.  We all have suffered a lot in covid lost lots of friend and family .But the worst of any situation  or pandemic is what's happening in Palestine now a whole area on the map of the world is being wiped and everyone has a closed eye over it . The Worker's Party and George Galloway is  a ray of sunshine and only voice which demands a cease fire . As a candidate, I’m strongly committed to championing justice and integrity above all else, ensuring that my friends and neighbours across Whalley Range and beyond feel seen, heard and supported.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Netherton and Holly Hall has some brilliant people, but the area isn’t getting the attention it deserves.
My opponents live here, and that’s fine, we all see the same issues every day.
The difference is how we fix them, not where we sleep.
If I’m elected, here’s what I’ll do:

✔ Cleaner, safer streets
How:
Work with residents to map fly-tipping hotspots
Push for targeted enforcement and named officers responsible
Press the council to use CCTV and fines properly, not as a PR line

✔ A council that listens
How:
Hold regular monthly surgeries in the ward
Give residents straight answers — no runaround
Publish what I’m doing so people can see progress

✔ Fairness for families and small businesses
How:
Chase repairs and landlord issues until they’re done, not ignored
Back local traders and markets instead of pointless vanity projects
Push back on endless charges being dumped on hard-working residents

I’m not in this for position or party titles.
I’m standing because this ward deserves someone who’ll graft for it every day, not just turn up for elections.
Netherton and Holly Hall deserve better and I’ll roll my sleeves up to deliver it.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Dear Residents,

National polling is consistent: **the majority of people want change**—and I think that reflects what many in our community are feeling too.
I’m Stephen Price, a local parent, a leader in the education sector, and your candidate in the upcoming election. My children have been educated here in Norton, I work mostly from my home in Oldswinford, and I’ve been a school governor at Redhill.
I am standing to be your councillor for Norton because I care about community. **I am committed to being a strong and effective representative**—someone who listens, brings fresh ideas, and works hard to deliver meaningful change.

- **An inclusive, transparent and collaborative approach**: I bring an open, inclusive, and collaborative approach to work. In my early career, as a scientist working on wildlife health, I worked with researchers from 16 countries and published reports with more than 100 other people. As a civil servant, after the pandemic, I was the technical lead for a major government data sharing project (between Ofqual, the DfE and UCAS). **I believe in working together to get things done and being open about how decisions are made. The Green Party doesn’t tell me how to vote.**
- **Leadership and transformation**: I have led teams for public and private organisations, building capability, engagement and shared ways of working. **I am good at finding new ways to do things that help organisations overcome challenges** because I lead on the design, delivery and communication of new solutions.
- **Making sure local people have their say and are proud of this area**: I am a board member of Stourbridge Community Land Trust, supporting the development of sustainable, comfortable, and affordable homes for local people. **I think our area is best served by working together as a community**, whether that be on housing, energy or transport.

You will be voting for one councillor representing Norton on Dudley council. **I can win here and will work hard to do the best job for you and our area.**

Yours sincerely,
Stephen Price
