Harpenden born and raised, St George's educated, and not going anywhere.

I am a training teaching assistant with experience within the pensions sector, meaning that I am well aware of the two major concerns for the average individual: what will happen to their future, and what will happen to the next generation's future.
Within the last twelve months, I have volunteered my time to the Harpenden Trust, working alongside many others to raise money and ensure that those people in our community who most need it get help. This has made it very clear to me just what matters to this community and just where its needs lie
.
When running for council in 2022-23, I was able to secure 26% of the vote with my first attempt at campaigning. This is what I am trying to protect. However, I also feel that there must be honesty – Harpenden needs to remain a town where the next generation can flourish and build their lives.
Our council is chronically under-represented when it comes to younger voices, and I'd like to change that — not to replace the experience already round the table, but to sit alongside it.

profile.statement_to_voters:
I’m standing to represent Broomfield and The Walthams on Chelmsford City Council because local people deserve a councillor who shows up and gets things done.

By 31, I had reached senior leadership level in a publicly listed company, leading teams, shaping strategy, and delivering results in a fast-moving, high-pressure environment. I’ve won industry awards, worked with well-known British brands, and led campaigns on an international stage. I know how to make decisions, take responsibility, and follow through.

Today, I run my own business, so I understand first-hand the challenges facing local companies - the rising costs, red tape, and need for practical
support.

But more importantly, I’m a parent raising my young son here in Chelmsford.

This is personal for me. I care deeply about the kind of place we’re building for the next generation.

If elected, I will focus on clear, local priorities:
- **Backing our high street and small businesses** — including pushing for an hour of free parking in the city centre to help bring life back to our high street.

- **Being visible and easy to reach** — with regular surgeries, attending parish council meetings, providing regular reports and a commitment to respond quickly and get things sorted.

- **Standing up for our villages** — working with Mike to continue the work already started to move traffic away from the villages of Great Waltham, Little Waltham and Broomfield, by making the main routes into Chelmsford use the planned new North East Bypass, the RDR and Northern RDR, and making the new Beaulieu Park station the first choice for commuters from the north of Chelmsford. Which is why we need more attractive parking charges at Beaulieu Park station.

- **Campaigning to reduce parking charges at Beaulieu Park Station** and improve taxi availability on its rank - the Lib Dem’s are making this station unusable.

I will also be a strong voice for protecting our green spaces, improving local services, and making sure development works for residents.

**I will be visible, accountable, and relentless in standing up for Broomfield and The Walthams—because this community deserves nothing less.**

If you have a local issues or concern you think I can help with, please do not hesitate to get in touch.

**Dear Resident**,

My name is Kevin, and I’m standing for you in Broomfield & Writtle division and Broomfield & The Walthams ward.

I live in Broomfield and work locally as a paramedic. Through my work, I see first-hand the pressures on our roads, services and community— I understand how much it matters that we feel safe, connected, and able to enjoy the natural world around us.

**A practical plan for our area**

I will focus on issues that really matter:

· Housing development that starts with services—schools, GP access, buses, and green spaces, and working with police and community safety partners

· Bus-first planning, protecting routes (including evenings and Sundays) and properly linking communities

· Safer school journeys, including a 20mph zone on School Lane and measures to reduce rat-running

· Proper road maintenance—because prevention is cheaper than repair, and residents shouldn’t pay twice

· Stronger support for social care, with reliable, well-commissioned services rather than patchwork provision

· Protection of wildlife and access to nature as a core part of planning decisions

· Transparency in local government and meaningful consultation—so residents are informed and have a real say in the decisions that affect them.

**A note on this election**

As the Green Party is largely funded by members and small donations, we have the freedom to stay focused on local priorities. By contrast, larger parties such as the Conservatives—and newer parties like Reform—rely heavily on large donors and corporations. I often urge voters to consider what interests those funding models could bring with them.

I notice too, that there has been some focus diverted to national issues like immigration. I want to be straight with voters: local councils do not set immigration policy. These elections are about our services, planning, transport, and community wellbeing. Suggesting otherwise risks distracting from the decisions that truly affect our day-to-day life here.

**A local representative you know**

In a time when trust in politics is uncertain, I’m offering something simple: a local candidate with a track record of serving the community every day.

I work with people from all walks of life, often at their most vulnerable. It has taught me how to listen, how to advocate, and how to work constructively with others to get things done.

That’s the approach I would bring to representing you.

This May, I hope you will consider supporting the Green Party.

**Yours sincerely**, 
Kevin 
Green Party Candidate Broomfield & Writtle / Broomfield & The Walthams

Our candidate for Broomfield and Writtle & for the by-election in Broomfield and The Walthams is Rachel Wheelhouse 👍

Rachel has lived in Broomfield for over 20 years, raised a family here and also worked at Broomfield Hospital, so is a true local champion. She is passionate about sorting potholes and pavements being accessible for all users, the environment, dealing with antisocial behaviour and championing local businesses and talent in arts and music.

On 7th May, vote for Rachel and the Lib Dems for a community champion ready to take on Tories and Reform.

ADDRESS TO VOTERS

My 2026 Address is online and can be found here: https://fionaradic.wordpress.com/2026/04/20/address-to-voters-3/

Beki Sellick is an engineer who worked through from shopfloor to Director. Her Peterborough business advises governments and companies on innovation. Beki helps deliver sustainable transport to fight climate change, bring people together and create jobs.

As a community campaigner, Beki creates a cleaner, safer, friendlier community. She’s won grants to build connections between neighbours.  She secured more power for tenants across Peterborough, improving homes by weeding out rogue landlords.

Beki rides her bike around the city where she lives and works with her family. Beki will protect our freedoms to live, love, work and study throughout a reformed EU, empowering local and national government get on with improving everyone’s lives. 

Beki is standing as “Liberal Democrat – To Stop Brexit”. Stopping Brexit is key to unlock our deadlocked politics and reverse the rise in Peterborough’s hate crime, isolation and poverty.

'Getting Brexit done' is false: 10 more years of uncertainty would follow. Our food safety, wages and health will be forced down as we try to get trade deals.

Negotiating another Brexit is false too, with more waste and divisiveness while we wait. But if the LibDems win, we can Stop Brexit before Christmas, Remain in the EU and invest the £50billion Remain Bonus in 3 things Peterborough really wants:

1. building up our city economy creating more skilled jobs, focusing on implementing innovation, giving every adult £10,000 to spend on learning;

2. reversing cuts with properly funded NHS and social care, police, transport and public services, targeting child poverty with free daycare from 9 months for working parents; and

3. tackling the climate emergency, insulating out fuel poverty and improving well-being.

Beki offers Peterborough a vote to Stop Brexit, so we can build a city and a country where every person, every community and our planet can thrive.

Coleridge has been my home for over 20 years, and I have had the privilege of being a teacher to many of our young people in local schools. I have been a local councillor for over 10 years. On 7 May, I am standing for re-election so that I can continue delivering for our communities here in Coleridge.

My priorities for Coleridge:

**Build communities, not just homes.** Tackle anti-social behaviour. Champion community grants, food hubs, holiday lunches. Build a new community room and improve green spaces.

**High quality homes for all.** Continue building a new generation of green council homes, including at Fanshawe Road, so that local people have great places to live. Support residents in need with housing and address rough sleeping.

**Cheaper, easier, safer travel.** Improve Cherry Hinton Road cycleway, tackle speeding cars, scooters and e-bikes, fix potholes, fight to save the Tiger Pass, champion better buses.

I’m already working hard for Coleridge. I’ve challenged poor planning decisions, and stood up for local businesses, including shops on Cherry Hinton Road. I’ve installed outdoor gym equipment. I made sure the number of council homes built on Fanshawe and Davy is more than before (up from 52 to at least 94). I brought in the Tiger Pass for young people and free pre-9.30am travel for concessionary bus pass holders.

Most of these things started as issues raised by fellow local residents like you. We have achieved all these improvements working together. But there’s so much more to do.

I want to continue to work with you to address other local issues and secure improvements that can benefit our local community and make Coleridge an even better place to live.

Tim is the local, hardworking choice for Coleridge.

Tim is a retired parent and IT Consultant.

Tim has lived in Cambridgeshire for 40 years; Coleridge is now his permanent residence.  Tim's favourite pastime is hiking, with my wife, anywhere in the world.

If elected Tim will work with Cllr Karen Young and Parliamentary Candidate Cheney Payne to fight for a fair deal for Coleridge.

During the pandemic, I set up a collection point for customers to support the local food bank and have helped to raise funds for people in need.

As the North West District President of the National Federation of Independent Retailers, I am also keen to get more support for local businesses.

I worked as a lecturer at West Lancashire College from 2009 to 2015 and I have worked training the next generation of teachers at Edge Hill University since 2015. I have taught hundreds of former students who now live and work around the council area. In 2010 I stood as the Green Party Parliamentary candidate for West Lancashire and again in the Parliamentary by-election in 2023.

As someone who has been a Green member for over 25 years, I've fought for social justice as an anti-racism campaigner on Merseyside, worked alongside anti-fracking campaigns and campaigning for a fairer society. As a father of three, my teenage children face a challenging world. When I was born CO2 was 327 parts per million (ppm) in the atmosphere - it is now at 429 ppm and rising - the climate crisis has never been more urgent.

There are those who deny science, stoke division in society and blame the poorest and the most vulnerable. There are politicians who are funded and influenced by the millionaires, billionaires and those who hold power in society, and bend to what they want. The Greens stand opposed to that.

We stand to represent local people and listen to concerns, for lower bills, for cleaner cheaper energy and to make hope normal again.

profile.statement_to_voters: Dave has lived in West End/Eastleigh Borough for over 20 years. He is a hardworking, community-minded candidate who has worked for the NHS since 2002 as a senior software engineer for University Hospital Southampton. He has served on the board of a number of local charities and chaired Eastleigh Green Party. Dave is the proud father of two (almost) grown-up boys and is passionate about building a sustainable, inclusive, and hope-filled society for them and future generations. He is also a musician, a YouTube content creator and has a keen interest in local history and heritage.

🙋‍♂️ Introducing Myself — Steve Humby, Reform UK Candidate for West End South

Hi everyone! I'm Steve Humby, and I'm standing as your Reform UK candidate for West End South on Eastleigh Borough Council and Hampshire County Council at the May 7th local elections.

I live in Hamble and have proudly called this corner of Hampshire home for 38 years. You might know me from my time serving the community — I spent 31 years as a Retained Firefighter and 8 years as a Wholetime Firefighter with Hampshire & IOW Fire and Rescue Service. I also served as a Special Constable based at Bitterne and Park Gate, and as an Ambulance Co-Responder. Public service has always been at the heart of who I am.

So what's brought me to politics? Honestly — frustration. A recent experience dealing with my Parish Council opened my eyes to just how painful and unnecessarily bureaucratic local government can be. If it felt that way to me, I can only imagine how it feels for other ordinary residents trying to get things done. That has to change.

If elected, my priorities will be:
✅ Tackling Eastleigh's growing council debt
✅ Helping ease the cost of living for local families
✅ Simply working for the people of West End — not for the system

I'm not a career politician. I'm a retired firefighter who has spent nearly four decades living and serving in this community. I'd love your support — and more than that, I'd love to hear what matters most to you.

👍 Follow my Facebook page to keep up with my campaign, and feel free to drop me a message anytime. Let's work together for West End — vote Steve Humby on May 7th!

#ReformUK #WestEndSouth #Eastleigh #Hampshire #StepUpForWestEnd #VoteSteveHumby

profile.statement_to_voters:
Hello I’m Julie Pattison, the Reform UK parliamentary candidate for Bolton South and Walkden. I have lived in Kearsley all my life and in 2018 I was a co-founder of Farnworth and Kearsley First, which was formed by a group of residents who were fed up of being let down by our local politicians and watching a once great area fall into decline. I joined Reform UK in 2022 out of sheer frustration over the treatment of our towns by the ruling parties. We have had a Labour MP for 12 years and seen no improvement or fight.

I have strong connections in Bolton South and Walkden through work, friends and family.

My working career has always been in the care/charity sectors. Being a lifelong learner and passionate about social justice, I have a deep sense of empathy for the needs of some of the most vulnerable in society and can identify with many people who are let down under the current system. Working with grass roots has given me the ability to see first-hand the impact our government has had on working families. We have all felt the negative impact of lack of access to GP's, NHS, housing, inflation (IE tax rises, energy rises), failing education and with no sign of improvement.

Any politician that stands proud to cut the red ribbon when they are announcing another food bank opening, should hang their heads in shame. It is for these reasons why I joined Reform, as I worry about what kind of future my grandchildren will have.

I am not a career politician; I am just a working-class lass from the North. I am doing this because I want better for our towns and future generations.

If I am blessed to be gifted your vote I promise to fight for improvement and be your voice.

I am a lecturer in biological sciences at Greater Manchester Medical school, helping to make this development the success it deserves to be for its students, for Farnworth, and for Bolton. I want to put the skills I have learnt in a career that has taken me to Africa, to Central Asia, and to China to use in improving Farnworth and Bolton.

I hold the Green values of environment, equality, and education to be vital to the development of places that strengthen the people that live in them in body, mind, and community. So that health and wellbeing come naturally not after a struggle against the way we are forced to live.

Communities that value everyone within them wherever they were born, whatever they believe, and whatever struggles they have overcome and continue to face. A society that enables all those within it to make the most of their own talents and abilities throughout their life.

locally. Having introduced Tadley Spring Clean week I
have continued to promote green issues now through
engagement with the Tadley Environment Group.

profile.statement_to_voters:
My name is Andrew Crichton, and I am your Labour County Council candidate in the Oxfordshire County Council elections on 1st May 2025.

Since I was first elected as your District Councillor in 2022, I have been trying to fix issues with our local infrastructure, adoptions of estates and advocate for residents with the County Council.

I have been left frustrated by a lack of engagement from current County Councillors for our area, and so I decided to stand to be your County Councillor on 1st May.

Hardwick residents should have a County Councillor who is proactive and contactable - but that simply isn't the case at the moment.

Please support me on 1st May and I will always be working on your side to try and improve things locally.

Simi Cumberbatch, a rooted, practical voice for Banbury, Hardwick

Simi Cumberbatch was born in Banbury and has spent her life rooted in the town she cares about. She lives locally, keeps an allotment, and deals with the same everyday issues as her neighbours, from potholes to stretched services and rising housing costs. When residents say worn roads make travel harder, that local infrastructure is under pressure, and that soaring house prices create uncertainty, Simi isn’t surprised. These are exactly the concerns people raise with her on the doorstep and the issues she is determined to tackle.

Simi brings a rare combination of environmental expertise, frontline healthcare experience and a hands‑on approach to community problems. Having worked in NHS pharmacy with GP surgeries and local hospitals, she understands the human stories behind the headlines: missed appointments, delayed treatments, and the stress that comes when services are at breaking point. In her current role in the environmental sector, she sees how everything connects such as planning, flooding, wildlife, transport and economic pressures and works to protect people, nature and local places. Her work includes reviewing environmental information, supporting flood‑resilience planning, safeguarding habitats and enabling sustainable, well‑planned growth.

Simi doesn’t just talk about community; she shows up for it. As a Banbury Flood Warden, she helps spot early warning signs and get problems dealt with before they grow. She volunteers on local litter picks and community improvement projects because she believes that consistent, practical action builds stronger, safer neighbourhoods. When sheds on her allotment site were burgled, Simi turned detective: she used tracking devices on her tools, helped the police recover stolen items, and showed calm under pressure, attention to detail and a willingness to stand up for her community.

Simi is standing to give Banbury Hardwick a stronger, better‑informed voice on the issues that matter most: fixing our failing infrastructure, protecting our town from flooding, safeguarding the green spaces we love, and making sure growth works for local people rather than pushing them out. If you want someone who understands Banbury from the ground up and will work tirelessly for residents, Simi Cumberbatch is ready to represent you.

Local Conservatives and I are campaigning for:

✓ Local infrastructure before new housing developments
✓ Freezing brown bin charges
✓ Protecting fortnightly bin collections
✓ Ending Cherwell’s pothole emergency
✓ A U-turn on appointment-only access to
recycling centres
✓ Tidying up our town centres

Cristina Palamini (she/they) is standing as a Green Party candidate because she believes Oxford has the talent and resources to be a greener, fairer city — and that what’s needed now is the political will to act. Claire Davies is the first elected Green Party councillor to Redditch Borough Council. She represents Webheath & Callow Hill ward where she holds regular surgeries, working with residents to help address issues they are facing or concerns that they have.

She has lived in the ward for the last 25 years, raising her 4 children here in Redditch and being an active member of community groups such as the Headless Cross Orchard.

“The natural world is something that is very important in my life, as is ensuring that we are being the best custodians of it as possible.”

These are a crucial set of local elections in Redditch. Having lived in the area all of my life, I know Redditch and Webheath and Callow Hill in particular deserve better. If elected, my priorities are clear:

Holding Developers to Account: Webheath has seen enough expansion without the proper infrastructure. I will ensure developers fulfill their promises to our community.

Protecting Green Spaces: Our local environment and parks like Morton Stanley is what makes this area a great place to live. I’ll fight to maintain and protect our parks and green spaces.

Ambition for Redditch: I’ll fight for the investment and bold vision our town deserves. From transport infrastructure to town centre regeneration, Redditch deserves better.

Redditch is a fantastic place to live and work, and I want to keep it that way. Vote Conservative on 7th May!

Successive Governments have cut public services, while the cost-of-living crisis deepens for working people, with energy, water and council tax bills all shooting up.

The Green Party is now the only progressive voice left in British politics; that’s why I’m standing for Mayor – I’m determined to drive meaningful change by:

• Ensuring development is carefully managed, prioritising the needs of the many over the few. 
• Delivering an integrated transport system, with more buses on more routes with simple and affordable fares. 
• Investing to end homelessness, fighting for tenants rights and campaigning for rent controls.
• Building more social housing because affordable housing simply isn’t. 
• Making sure housing developments include community facilities, and homes are properly insulated.
• Taking action to resolve the looming water crisis, and making more space for nature.
• Increasing support for small businesses, and growing the green economy.

Having directed the Cambridge office of a national construction company for 15 years, I understand business. I’m passionate about sustainability, and the importance of developing the skills of local people. Now retired, I work for social enterprises supporting the homeless and getting young people into construction.

Polls consistently report that most of us want to see the richest taxed and we want more government action on the climate crisis. But the major parties are not listening. The Greens are! If you want real hope and real change, VOTE GREEN on Thursday 1st May.

I became a member of the Green Party because I strongly believe in equality, community and the environment.

I have lived in Worthing for 13 years, working locally in health and social care. I currently work in the NHS as an Occupational therapist.

I previously worked in the homeless sector and  have seen first hand the impact of the lack of housing for local people- families and people with disabilities living in temporary accommodation out of area away from their families and social networks. We need to do more for our people unfortunately experiencing unstable housing.

I am also passionate about building community spaces in Worthing. I was a volunteer director and fundraiser for the alternative arts venue and social enterprise Cellar Arts Club. I also coordinated and hosted music events One Love Worthing, celebrating diversity and community support groups.

Key Issues I would focus on as a councillor:

- Housing for all – holding developers to account
- Community to build unity and opportunity
- Community wealth development- sourcing investment locally to build our economy.

My name is Bradley Rowatt and I’m standing to be your Conservative councillor on the 7th of May.

Our priorities are to benefit the lives of those in Durrington and Worthing as a whole - which Labour has forgotten both nationally and  locally

Firstly, I will stand against any housing of illegal migrants. Worthing will NOT become another dumping ground like Crowborough.

Secondly, I will stand against decline - Our pavements and roads have been neglected for over four years causing them to now become a blight on our town.

Thirdly, I will support efforts to crack down on crime. Shoplifting is now running rampant in Worthing. I will work with our PCC - Katy Bourne - to punish repeat offenders to bring back safety in our streets.

I will fight for lower council tax and bring an end to labours CHRONIC overspending.

IF YOU AGREE WITH ME. Vote Bradley on the 7th of May.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Trevor Lloyd-Jones went to school and grew up in Yateley in Northeast Hampshire from childhood. Former school governor specialising in data, education KPIs and performance, he has experience managing small and very large businesses with a lifetime career in publishing, marketing, and the digital and technology industries. Former Conservative and former Conservative candidate Trevor is standing on a true reform agenda and a ‘small c’ conservative agenda: reform of the NHS, the judiciary, tax, with a pro-growth economic plan, and a new deal for young people on student debt and on home ownership.

Trevor is campaigning against cuts to the armed forces, the hollowing out of Britain’s defence sector and this government’s shoddy treatment of veterans. He was one of the Brexit Party candidates who stood aside for the Boris Johnson intake of Conservatives at the 2019 election.

In the media and locally Trevor is lobbying passionately for a new kind of bottom-up/bubble-up politics, an open, more ambitious and accessible politics. He speaks out against undemocratic and faceless global organisations taking the place of our democratic institutions.

But now, after more than a decade of the Conservatives the public have become conditioned to accept failure. They’ve become used to ministers blaming everyone but themselves, whether it’s the House of Lords, immigration lawyers, EU bureaucrats or the European Court of Human Rights. In the media and locally Trevor is lobbying passionately for a new kind of bottom-up/bubble-up politics, an open, more ambitious and accessible politics. He speaks out against undemocratic and faceless global organisations taking the place of our democratic institutions.

When not on the campaign trail Trevor can be found enjoying countryside walks or writing books on philosophy, politics and 'conscious capitalism'. He holds a degree in Economics and Public Administration from University of London and a Masters degree in Business Administration.

Lisa, who has proudly called Yateley home for well over 20 years, and the local area for over 40 years, is standing as your Conservative candidate for Yateley West on 7 May.

A proven community champion, as founder and chair of Back Off Berkeley Homes she continues to lead opposition to Firgrove Farm and other green gap developments affecting Yateley. She will also prioritise local services and tackling crime and anti-social behaviour.

Lisa is known locally for her active campaigning against the controversial planning application for Firgrove Farm, as well as other inappropriate local green gap development.  She is the Founder, and Chair, of the Back Off Berkeley Homes action group, working closely with other community organisations to fight this and other local planning issues.
  
Lisa currently works within education as a key worker.  She is also a registered therapist.  Prior to this, Lisa was an international corporate communications specialist working both within industry and with her own consultancy.  During this time, she worked closely with both national and international governments and politicians, including working within challenging geopolitical environments.
 
 Lisa is passionate about protecting our local green spaces, wildlife and environment.  Her priorities are fighting to retain the character of Yateley West, not just for our generation, but for many more  to come.  Lisa is also hoping to tackle, alongside local agencies, the anti-social behaviour that can blight the lives of local residents.  This includes intimidating behaviour within our town centre, local streets and recreation areas, as well as wildlife crime, and unlicenced vehicles disturbing residents use of common land.
 
 Lisa looks to bring a new perspective to representing the community that Yateley appears to have lost under LibDem control.  She plans to bring much more active public representation for constituents.  She is here to listen to, and represent, what local residents want most.

I am standing as an independent candidate for the CDC ward of Cross & Neithrop. I believe that people are disillusioned with party politics and are eager for change. I will make decisions based upon facts and not party affiliation or the whims of a donor.

I feel that to make Banbury a better place to live we need to look at the following areas. 
Poverty: the cost of living has increased for everyone, but it affects the poorest and most vulnerable the most. We should provide more equity so that everyone gets the tools and resources they need to succeed.

I am standing as a Green Party councillor in Banbury Cross and Neithrop because I am passionate about doing the best for our local area.

Having listened to many Banbury locals, I understand the following as key areas of concern: 
• terrible road surfaces and potholes, 
• keeping and improving the Horton Hospital, 
• empty shops on out Highstreet and in Castle Quay shopping centre 
• struggling with capacity in our schools for growing numbers of children 
• poorly planned roadworks
• a lack of activities, places and spaces for our young people.

All of these are key areas of concern for locals and in my capacity as a District Councillor, I promise to work hard with the necessary authorities to tackle these issues.

profile.statement_to_voters:
I’m Kat Bull, the candidate for Avenue Ward and Fareham Titchfield Division. I run a bar on Fareham’s West Street (West Street Alehouse) and have seen the effects a strong community can have on a group of people.

I have owned the Alehouse with my husband since it opened 7 years ago putting us at the heart of Fareham’s Community and Independent Shopping Scene. And I have lived here since 2020, and before that I grew up in Gosport so I am a local gal with local focus!

Some of the things I care deeply about are youth services and schemes to get young people supported and given the chance to do something positive for themselves and their communities.
I also strongly stand behind fair and affordable housing for all. As a long time-renter with small children I know how scary and difficult that can be while also being the only option for many.
I believe if the community is at the heart and focus of everything we do, we can’t fail. When people support and uplift each other everyone thrives. I truly believe I am a lifter.

I am proud to be standing as the Reform UK candidate for The Avenue ward in Fareham.
I have lived locally in Stubbington, Titchfield and Fareham for the past 11 years, and I care deeply about our community and the issues that affect residents every day.
For the last 9 years, I have run my own business, helping people find work and supporting local employers. Earlier in my career, I worked as both a driving instructor and a bus driver, giving me a real understanding of the challenges people face on our roads and in everyday life.
I am standing for Reform UK because I believe local people deserve better representation, more transparency, and real value for money from the council. I am currently supporting the Reform candidate in Fareham Park in opposing the proposed closure of Henry Cort School, which I believe is an important part of our local community.
My priorities for The Avenue ward include:
Improving our roads and tackling ongoing traffic and maintenance issues
Addressing crime and helping residents feel safer in their neighbourhoods
Ensuring council spending is responsible, transparent, and focused on local needs
Delivering better, more reliable local services for residents
I believe in common sense, accountability, and standing up for local people. If elected, I will work hard to ensure residents’ voices are heard and that The Avenue ward gets the attention and investment it deserves.

I have been front and centre in these issues, working on your behalf, not just during election time but every day.

profile.statement_to_voters:
As a Castle resident for 23 years, with children educated at Mayfield and Chesterton, I understand our community and am proud to serve you as your Labour City Councillor. On 7th May, I am seeking your support for re-election to continue that work.
Over the past five years, I have worked full time as your Councillor to improve local services, strengthen community safety and promote tolerance across our diverse neighbourhoods. I am committed to restoring our natural environment, protecting chalk streams, achieving sewage-free rivers, and tackling climate change. I have decades of experience in local government, planning and development. Over the last three years as Cabinet Member for Finance and Resources I have turned a forecast deficit into a balanced budget.

**They want to confuse you by endorsing all kinds of weird causes, so you forget what's really at stake: you.**

As the Greens, LibDems and Labour, the parties that dominate Cambridge, sink more and more into populist stunts and extreme ideology, what do you think about it? What hopes can you have for your future, your family’s future, your security...when it all lies in the hands of people who are trying to impress their fans through irrational, foolish acts bordering on sedition?

How safe are your interests in their hands? While you despair about the potholes, crime and dismal infrastructure in your areas, they are screaming at demonstrations in solidarity with various dodgy overseas organisations and regimes, dancing with strippers, clowning around.

I’ve spoken to a lot of people in our ward who are very worried. The only way to overcome this is: **don’t put your future in their hands.**

You've voted for them for years, but lately they have grown feral. Your needs, your concerns, your future? Not interesting. They have bigger fish to fry. Have you seen their antics? The things they say, sometimes behind closed doors, sometimes not? The people they associate with? Do you still think you can trust them? Perhaps you haven’t seen or heard? Would you care to look into it? You’ll be shocked! Do your research, ask around: it’s all out there. Time for a change, before it’s too late. Democracy is not about picking your favourite team or colour. It’s about ensuring you are represented by people who will fight your corner.

Trust me, I’m a doctor. I’ve been fighting for people’s lives for 25 years.

Vote for me, Dr. Khalid, on 7 May 2026. I’m not for any party. I’m Independent. For Castle, for our community, our families, our future.

1. I am against reintroducing congestion charges.
2. I will defend and maintain green spaces and children's playgrounds.
3. I will curb antisocial behaviour, e.g. very fast and dangerous driving in Cambridge.

Luke first moved to Cambridge for university after growing up in Northern Ireland.

He returned to the city after a year working in Brussels, and decided to make Castle his home on Windsor Road.

In Northern Ireland, Luke was involved in campaigns to improve young people’s mental health, mitigate the impact of Brexit, and build a shared community.

Luke works in Cambridge University supporting young people from under- privileged backgrounds to access higher education.

Luke also volunteers with a local scout group, enabling young people to  gain skills for life. From working in Brussels on UK-EU affairs, Luke knows the harm Brexit has done, and is a tireless advocate for rejoining the EU.

Luke loves the green spaces of Castle and our vibrant community. You’ll often spot him at ParkRun at Eddington Lake or catching up with friends at the Castle Inn.

As a local resident, Luke is delighted to be standing up for his neighbours in Castle Ward as the Lib Dem candidate in May’s election.

I originally moved to Cambridge to study psychology and quickly grew to love this city and community. Now, as your Green Party candidate for Castle ward, I am campaigning for social and environmental justice.

Cambridge is a city of incredible resources and affluence, but remains one of the most unequal cities in the UK. We need to work harder to ensure that everyone in our community benefits from that success. Bridging the “town and gown” divide by breaking down barriers between the university and local residents will lead to a more united and fairer Cambridge. I am also dedicated to protecting our vital green spaces in Castle and across Cambridge. These areas are the lifeblood of our local community and must be protected as such.

In the May 2023 Council elections, Labour came a close second place behind the Conservatives in Dunsmore.

Labour has consistently led the fight against Conservative plans to launch a new Local Plan. We believe that services for existing residents such as better health, education, leisure and social care must be improved as the priority before anything else. The last 20 years has seen Rugby witness the highest rates of house building and warehouse development in the Midlands.

Having spent a career as a senior project manager in local government Alison is able to offer real life expertise in helping the lives of her constituents. Away from work, Alison's spare time is spent working to enhance environmental standards in Rugby and pursuing her passion for the arts and live music.

I was born and raised in West London and moved to the West Midlands shortly after the 2008 recession. My experiences in a diverse socio-economic environment have fuelled my passion for individual liberties, personal responsibility, and limited government intervention.

Early Life and Education

I hold an Engineering degree, and, for a long time, I lived my life without paying much more attention to politics than most people. My interest in politics was piqued by the Brexit debate. It seemed like a clear choice, yet the government's handling of the issues made me sit up and pay attention. Since then, I've delved into political philosophy, particularly the works of Ludwig von Mises, Thomas Sowell, Ron Paul, Friedrich Hayek, and Milton Friedman. These intellectual pursuits have solidified my belief in the importance of free markets and individual freedoms as foundations of a prosperous society.

Career and Political Involvement

After graduation, I embarked on a career as a consultant engineer, gaining firsthand experience of the regulatory challenges faced by businesses. This professional background significantly shaped my advocacy for deregulation and economic liberalization.  My transition into politics was driven by a desire to combat what I perceive as the overreach of government into citizens' lives and economic activities, exemplified by the events of 2020.

I first entered the political arena by standing for local council elections. In 2023, I stood for Reform UK, attracted by the party's commitment to challenging the status quo and its platform on Brexit and mass immigration. Now, I am standing for the first time as a parliamentary candidate, aiming to bring my libertarian principles to the national stage.

Political Platform and Vision

My political platform is built on the pillars of personal liberty, economic freedom, and pragmatic governance. I advocate for significant tax cuts, reduction in public spending, and the dismantling of unnecessary regulations that stifle entrepreneurship and innovation. My vision includes a reformed welfare system focused on empowering individuals through work incentives rather than dependency.

On social issues, I am a staunch defender of civil liberties. I support policies that protect freedom of speech, privacy rights, and individual autonomy.

Prospects

My blend of professional expertise and libertarian principles positions me as a transformative candidate within Reform UK. As I step onto the national stage, my vision for a leaner, more responsive government resonates with an increasing number of voters disillusioned with traditional political offerings. I am not just a candidate; I am part of a movement for a freer, more prosperous Britain.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Hey, I'm Lex, and I’m standing for Pendle and Clitheroe because I believe that our country has been let down by the two main parties, and the Liberal Democrats demonstrated in 2010 that they can’t be trusted either.

The Green Party is the only viable party to keep the next government in check, and I will put the constituency and its people above the political shenanigans we’ve witnessed over the years.

It's embarrassing that only 25% of the Levelling Up fund has been spent in the North, demonstrating once again that we're either forgotten about, or the North is just not cared about. A vote for the Green Party would be a vote for investment in the North of the UK, where we'd aim to tackle all of the problems the constituency currently faces:

- The Cost of Living for many on low wages and high levels of poverty especially of children.

- Shortage of affordable houses to rent or buy especially for young people and coupled trying to set up independent living.

- Fear of being made homeless by no fault evictions or foreclosure of mortgages.

- Failure to access NHS care when they need help now with a long waiting list to be treated.

- Lack of Social Care homes with enough staff, which is creating bed blocking in Hospital.

- Poor connected rail and bus transport, accessible to people on low incomes and those young people in isolated rural areas, who cannot afford their own means of transport.

The Green Party recognises these problems and it's why our main policies are around tackling the Climate Emergency by investing in green jobs and public services which will reduce the cost of living and increase the incomes of citizens.

This investment in people, services and the environment will be paid for by raising the money needed primarily from a wealth tax on rich people and windfall taxes from polluting private companies.

profile.statement_to_voters:
I’m Tom Atkin-Withers, a local resident just like you, and your Green Party candidate for Graves Park in the upcoming 7th May local elections.

I’m a charity trustee working with young people, and through that work I see first-hand the challenges facing families in our area, across our city, and nationally. I’m also an active member of the Graves Park community, and what I hear most often on the doorstep is frustration about poor and inaccessible public transport across the ward, and despair that people aren’t being listened to. Frankly, it makes me as angry as it makes you.

**I actively stand up for Graves Park**

The current Labour and Liberal Democrat councillors have failed to stand up for Graves Park residents. I want to change that. Over the last year alone, driven by concerns raised by local people, I have:
- Worked with Green councillors to push for better transport links between Graves Park and Chesterfield Hospital
- Secured repairs to damaged bus stops across our area
- Helped secure the prompt resurfacing of the new crossing by Meadowhead School after a resident was injured
- Launched a petition to get Osmaston Road adopted and resurfaced
- Worked to keep our green spaces litter free

**Unlike other parties Greens drive change**

While Labour and the Lib Dems keep things the same, the Greens are working hard to improve the city we all love. From Climate Bonds that allow residents to fund solar panels on our children’s schools, to protecting our environment from toxic pesticides and fighting for better-funded public transport, we’re focused on practical action that improves everyday life.

**I can win here and bring hope back to Sheffield.**

Neighbouring wards like Gleadless Valley, Nether Edge & Sharrow, and Ecclesall already have Green councillors, and across the country the Green Party is growing as more people respond to a hopeful, honest alternative to politics as usual.

As your councillor, my priority would be simple: delivering local results. Standing up for our communities, protecting public services, creating secure green jobs, and making sure everyone has access to decent housing, clean air,
and a strong local economy.

**Sheffield’s future should be shaped by the people who live here. Together, we can build it.**

I'm Julia, I work in social housing, enabling elderly and vulnerable residents to receive the support they need.
I'm standing for Council to give something back to the city and community that raised and supported me and my family, and to ensure future generations have the same opportunities that Sheffield gave me. I want to be part of a Council that is proud to provide good quality housing and improves the quality of life and the prospects for all its residents.
I've been out listening on the doorstep, and at events, to hear what is important to you and how I can support you all. I've taken part in community events in Graves Park and Woodseats and I have been working alongside your Labour Councillor Rob Bannister. I'm committed to working hard for everyone in our area.
I am passionate about improving opportunities and living standards for all. As a Labour councillor I would be able to work with stakeholders to challenge inequalities and put policies into place to remove discrimination and offer support.

Graves Park Ward – Community First Manifesto

Putting Residents Back at the Heart of Decisions

Graves Park Ward is one of the most valued and distinctive parts of Sheffield. From its green spaces to its strong community spirit, it deserves leadership that listens, protects, and delivers.
This campaign is built on a simple promise: local people come first.

1. Protecting Our Quality of Life
Residents deserve to feel proud of where they live. That means tackling the everyday issues that affect daily life:
* Safer streets – Working with police and local groups to reduce anti-social behaviour and improve community safety
* Cleaner neighbourhoods – Prioritising street cleaning, tackling fly-tipping, and improving waste services
* Smarter traffic solutions – Addressing congestion, speeding, and unsafe junctions with practical, locally informed measures
Our goal: A cleaner, safer, and more liveable Graves Park Ward for everyone.

2. Defending the Character of Our Area
Graves Park Ward is unique – and that must be protected.
* Opposing overdevelopment that erodes green space or local identity
* Ensuring planning decisions respect the history and character of our communities
* Protecting valued public spaces for future generations
Our commitment: No change without respect for what makes our area special.

3. Real Community Voice in Council Decisions
Too many residents feel decisions are made about them, not with them.
* Introducing genuine public consultation on major local decisions
* Giving residents a stronger voice in planning and development
* Ensuring local concerns are heard early—not ignored until it’s too late
Our promise: You will be listened to—and your views will matter.

4. Restoring Trust and Transparency
Trust in the council has been damaged by secrecy and unclear decision-making.
* Ending “behind closed doors” decisions wherever possible
* Publishing clear, accessible information on council plans and land use
* Holding decision-makers accountable to residents
Our pledge: Open, honest, and accountable local government.

5. Protecting Our Public Assets – Especially Graves Park
Our parks and public land belong to the community—not to private interests.
* Defending publicly owned land from inappropriate sale or privatisation
* Ensuring Graves Park is protected, properly maintained, and accessible to all
* Establishing clear safeguards on how public land can be used
Our position: Public land must stay public.

6. Resolving the Norton Nurseries Dispute
The ongoing situation at Norton Nurseries has caused deep concern and division.
* Calling for a fair, transparent resolution
* Ensuring all decisions are made in the public interest
* Bringing the community into the process to rebuild trust
Our aim: A solution that respects both the community and the long-term future of the site.

7. No to Asset Stripping – Yes to Investment
Residents are right to be concerned about the future of local assets.
* Opposing the sell-off of community land and facilities
* Pushing for proper investment in maintenance and improvement
* Ensuring long-term planning, not short-term financial decisions
Our belief: You don’t fix finances by selling off the future.

8. Investing in What Matters
Neglect costs more in the long run. We will:
* Fight for better funding for parks, roads, and public spaces
* Ensure maintenance is proactive—not reactive
* Support community-led initiatives that improve the area
Our focus: Sustainable investment that benefits everyone.

A Fresh Start for Graves Park Ward
This manifesto is about restoring balance:
* Between development and protection
* Between the council and the community
* Between short-term decisions and long-term wellbeing
Together, we can protect what matters and build a better future for Graves Park Ward.

Protecting our ward, restoring trust, and standing up for our community.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Yetunde Adeshile is the Conservative Party candidate for Nethermayne Ward in the Basildon Borough Council, and Gloucester Park Division for the Essex County Council elections on 7th May 2026. 
Yetunde has been a local resident of Basildon for over 20 years and understands local issues, which is why she is standing as a candidate for the ward and the division.

Since 2014, Yetunde has supported under privileged people in the Borough, particularly young people, families and those from ethnic community. She has provided training and development opportunities which has helped participants gain higher academic grades, start meaningful employment, business or improve their business ethics. She delivers community cohesion projects that improve cultural integration relationships locally. She is part of the Basildon business community.

Yetunde is passionate about the development of our community. She would welcome the opportunity to be the voice that will help Basildon achieve the changes that we need for our Borough. It her passion that drives her dire to serve and work hard to move Basildon forward in the right direction. When elected, I pledge to serve Basildon community with integrity, dedication, and genuine commitment to our community. I will show up and be the strong voice that you need.

7th May 2026 Vote Yetunde Adeshile. The string voice for our community, advocating for a united and brighter future for Basildon, as we make Britain Great Again.

candidates.ballot_description: Independent Trade Union and Socialist Candidate
candidates.name: Chris Huggins

profile.statement_to_voters:
I am a father and full time carer to my disabled son, I have experienced the consequences of cuts to public services and know that Basildon deserves better! I will fight for funding for roads, education and safe green spaces, as well as warm, affordable homes.

Like many SEN parents in Essex, I am concerned about the lack of resources and spaces at special needs schools. We are currently waiting on a SEND Tribunal in 2027 to contest the councils refusal to allocate my sons placement. SEND Tribunals rule 98% of cases in favour of the parents. In 2022, Essex County Council spent £566,299 fighting cases they know they are going to lose. If elected, I will fight to stop this waste of time and money and propose this money is spent on Class spaces. I will fight for you as parents to help you get the education your child deserves. Opening up more spaces in special need schools will then help mainstream education as teachers are spending more time on education for all children.

With my background of Automotive Engineering and mechanics, I am concerned with the potholes and the ‘fixes’ done in Basildon. I will fight to have our roads fixed properly rather than quick fixes to potholes that have come back when winter comes round. Our road are being neglected and require more than filling in the potholes. Potholes are having a big environmental impact with the increased amount of tyres and car parts needing changed sooner and the extra fuel used.

I will fight to protect our green spaces and help make them accessible for all. To make environmental conservation a key goal. Our green areas also have a huge impact on our mental health and wellbeing. I will help keep the local environment on the agenda to help keep us happier and healthier. Keeping green spaces available to all and safe is a huge priority to me.

Basildon needs more Council homes, which I will push for. Luxury homes owned by foreign business interests are taking money out of the Essex economy. Many people feel that these developments are to line councillors pockets and do not help with the housing crisis. Many residents find themselves evicted by private landlords and the council will not help unless they are homeless. More and more people are forced into temporary accommodation outsourced and being charged exorbitant fees to Basildon council. We need to stop these predatory landlords milking off the council taxpayer. I will push for building mainly social housing, end of using private landlords and improve help for people facing eviction.

profile.statement_to_voters:
I’m Marieanne, I live locally with my children and cats, and I share your ambitions and your frustrations for Heeley, Meersbrook and Gleadless Valley. This May, I am standing for re-election and I hope to continue working with my Green colleagues Alexi Dimond and Paul Turpin.

It is a challenging time with successive governments continuing austerity and the rising cost of living – that’s why Greens work in our communities, for our communities, year-round – not just when there is a vote to be won.

Greens offer a different kind of politics – one rooted in hope, integrity, compassion and action. For me, this is a commitment to the place I call home, standing up for people and all our different communities here.

**Action Where It Matters**

As your Green Councillor over the last four years, I have focused on positive action across our area, such as:

- Help with Cost of Living: Championing “Income Maximisation”; bringing free face-to-face advice to our community centres in Heeley Green and Newfield Green to help people get lower bills. 
- Supporting residents: Casework to help people through homelessness, timely housing repairs and maintenance in Gleadless Valley and more action on street repairs like potholes across the ward.
- Improving our environment: Acting on issues in parks, woodlands, waterways and highways. Pushing for more spaces for young people, school streets and safe routes to school.
- Community action: Supporting play streets and yard sales. Collaborating with amazing grass roots organisations on community skip days, river clean ups, girls football in Herdings Park, nature recovery in Meersbrook Park and backing community ambitions for Meersbrook Hall.

I am pushing for investment in Gleadless Valley and for solar energy on homes, schools and new leisure centres. I have challenged poor planning applications, spoken out for disabled people and carers, called for ethical investment and championed beaver reintroduction and nature recovery initiatives.

It’s been a real privilege to be your Councillor for the last four years and I hope you will put your trust in me to continue to represent you and our communities. *I will, as always, do my best for you*.

Thank you

profile.statement_to_voters:
I have lived in Stevenage since 1956 and have grown up in the town, attending Longmeadow Infant and Junior Schools and Barnwell when it was a Secondary Modern School. 

I married in 1975, had two children, a girl and a boy, who attended local Schools and then when onto Universities. 

I have previously been a Stevenage Borough Councillor for a total of 28 years, serving on many committees, which has given me a wealth of knowledge and experience within Local Government, which I can take forward at County level. 

During my time as a Borough Councillor I also had the honour of being The Mayor twice-2018-2019 and 2022-2023. Carrying out many Civic duties and engaging with the many wonderful organisations throughout our town. I have also for many years been a member of some of these organisations, The Stevenage, Ingelheim, Autun Twinning Association. The Stevenage Kadoma Link and a local Community Association. I have recently become a member of a Primary Care Patient Participation Group and a Trustee of the Stevenage District Scouts. 

I have in the past been a School Governor, serving for over forty years. 

I enjoy spending my free time with family and friends and supporting the Performing Arts.

I first moved to Stevenage with my parents back in 1967 and have been in love with our town ever since.

Growing up in the town, I became aware of the great sense of togetherness and community we had and it's saddening to now see people working actively to take that away from us.

I initially became involved in local politics in the 1970s and have been an active member of the local Green Party since 2015. As Woodfield's first Green councillor I will be there to listen to you and represent your views, especially regarding the social and environmental issues currently prominent in the area.

I grew up in Hertfordshire and I moved to Stevenage a few years ago, looking for a quiet spot to live with my two rescue cats Indy and Nala.

I am a lawyer working in criminal justice and I prosecute and defend in some of the most serious cases, including as a specialist in organised crime and rape and serious sexual offences. I volunteer to support those from disadvantaged backgrounds in gaining entry to the law.

If I was elected as your councillor I would advocate for your needs and improving your local area and access to essential services.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Hi I’m Christine and I am proud to be standing as the Wolds Ward candidate in the upcoming May local elections. I wish to be a member of a strong, experienced team that has the ability to deliver for you.

Here are just some of my key pledges to residents which I feel are so important in protecting the rural character and delivering on what our area stands for - our strong sense of community spirit and pride.

Protecting our rural villages

I will stand with residents in opposing inappropriate large-scale development that threatens our countryside, landscapes and village identities.
If elected, I will stand up and will champion residents strong opposition and concerns in the council chamber when it comes to planning matters such as the Freshney Valley/ Grimsby West housing development proposals.

Continue the positive re-generation

In recent years under the Conservative administration we have overseen some of the largest re-generation projects our area has ever seen. Our local towns of Grimsby and Cleethorpes have seen constructions being erected with the majority of projects due to be completed by the end of 2027. Let's continue the positive.

Supporting our environment

I will push for action in flooding areas such as around the Great Coates Road area, support nature restoration around our borough via various new scheme proposals and protect our lovely green spaces. Wolds ward is the home of a rare chalk stream, ancient community churches, and rolling green hills. By working with local partners and organisations like Natural England, I will ensure these are protected for future generations to enjoy.

Working for you

I believe in being visible, accessible and accountable. All councillors should uphold these principles in public life and I promise to always be there for you.
I will hold regular surgeries, publish ward updates with regular newsletters, work closely with our fantastic Parish Councils, and respond promptly to residents' concerns.

We must protect our rural villages, natural heritage, the next generation of young people and demand common sense solutions that work for all.

SAY YES TO PUTTING PEOPLE BEFORE PARTY POLITICS
SAY YES TO BEING VISIBLE AND ACCOUNTABLE
SAY YES TO FIGHTING OVER-DEVELOPMENT IN OUR VILLAGES

Philip Thomas moved to Exeter five years ago and has lived in Priory Ward for four of those years. He works as a physicist at the University of Exeter and volunteers at his local church.

Philip is a strong supporter of transparent, collaborative decision making in local government. If elected councillor, one of his priorities will be to work hard to improve the Council's transparency and accountability. He has spoken to other Priory Ward residents who feel taken for granted by the Labour Council, for example regarding the handling of the development of Edwards Court.

I’m Jack Vickers, your local Green candidate for Priory. I’m proud to be standing for election on 7 May.

It’s been great meeting so many of you, and I’m very grateful for the support people continue to show me here. Speaking with residents on the doorstep has helped me better understand the issues people face day to day. Here is some important information ahead of the election in a few weeks.

I lived on Burnthouse Lane for years, and if there’s one thing I remember about living in the area, it’s the widespread feeling that we’d been neglected and forgotten by the council. Too often our neighbourhood hasn’t had the attention or investment it deserves, and many residents tell me they feel the same today.
I have worked many jobs over the years, from warehousing to retail to security, and I know how to work in a team to deliver results.

I am passionate about tackling the lack of affordable housing in our city, as well as improving transport and cycling infrastructure. I am also committed to protecting our green spaces, such as Ludwell Valley Park, so they can be enjoyed by local families for generations to come.

I am proud to have been born and raised in Exeter, where my family has deep roots. Having lived here all my life, I care deeply about our community’s future. My experience travelling, running a business, and working as a ski teacher and national coach has helped me connect with people from all walks of life. I am standing as a councillor to ensure local voices are heard. I will be approachable, accountable, and focused on practical solutions. I am not a career politician—I am one of you, committed to making a difference.

profile.statement_to_voters:
My name is John Francis Gilling, and I will be standing as an independent candidate for the Orchard Park Ward on 7th May.

Why Independent?

- Because I am fed up with the restrictions that come with party politics. The only line I would want to tow is that of the people who might vote for me.
- I feel that the existing councillors in Orchard Park have been in for far too long and have become complacent. In fact I am unsure if any of them actually live in the area.

- I feel that in General, Party Politics has become full of recycled MP'S and Councillors that flit from one Political Party to the next, which doesn't inspire me to feel that they will stand by their promises or their principles.

Why Orchard Park

- Because I have lived in this area for the last 20 years, and I care about my community, the area, and the people.

- I am fed up with seeing this area neglected and the negative press it gets from the media etc.

What can I do if I get elected

- Unlike candidates of the past I will not make false promises that I have no control over of delivering.

-Therefore all I will promise you is that I will put 💯 per cent effort into being a voice for you in the local council. I will listen to you, I will speak up for you, and I will represent the people of Orchard Park Ward to the Best of my ability.

- Because I live in the area I will be visual, recognised, and you will know who I am.

- I will not be hiding away in a house elsewhere in the City if the going gets tough.

Do I think I have a chance of being elected.

- If I didn't feel I had a chance I wouldn't waste my, or yours, time by standing as a candidate.

- I stood in the last local elections 2 years ago as an independent, and I came 3rd with 169 votes, Labour won with 999 votes. But the most interesting stat for me is that out of about 10,000 eligible voters less than 2000 bothered to vote. Which means the biggest majority is the non voters, as is echoed right across the country, and that apathy is the main reason the same people and political parties keep getting elected and having control of our lives.

- That tells that people have lost faith in the Political Parties, and that they want a different option to vote for. If you are one of these people why not give me a chance, and vote for me?

Hi, I’m Amazon.
I was born and raised in Hull and work for the NHS. I care deeply about our city and its communities having previously been awarded a Civic Crown award for my services working to raise awareness of certain marginalised communities. I’m a keen gardener and I also occasionally find the time to perform stand-up comedy, which I see more as a hobby than I do a career aspiration.

I always speak up when something isn’t right and will always put myself on the line when vital action is needed. It would be an honour to represent the people of Orchard Park on behalf of the Green Party and I’m excited to do all that I can in the short time we have left before the local elections.

Hi there, I'm so excited to introduce myself! My name is Jolene Hills and I'm a proud entrepreneur who has owned and operated multiple businesses in Southend. My mother is one of my biggest inspirations and she has shown me the business ropes having run many across Southend.  One of my recent ventures is the Cobham Hotel in Westcliff, which I acquired just before lockdown which, as you can imagine, was a difficult time.  However, we successfully got through it and I am proud to see the business thriving.

​

I have 2 children who I adore and who I want to grow up in a city that will give them the best chance of being happy and healthy.  As it happens, one of my daughters is studying politics so she will always be on hand if needed!  

​

As a born-and-bred Southend resident, I've always had a deep love for our city. However, I couldn't help but notice that there were areas for improvement. For example, I strongly believe that Southend Pier, one of the town's most iconic landmarks, needs more at the end of it.  Perhaps I am pointing out the obvious!

 

As a can-do person, I'm always eager to roll up my sleeves and tackle any challenge head-on.  I manage on the ground-floor, so I am used to dealing with all manner of people issues.  It is this experience I hope to bring to the residents of St Luke's.

​

I am thrilled to be getting more involved in my community to see what I can do to help. I believe that by working together, we can make Southend an even better place to live, work, and visit. So if you're looking for a passionate and dedicated partner to help you make a positive impact, look no further. I can't wait to see what we can accomplish together!

​

I am enormously excited to be the St Luke's Confelicity candidate and look forward to meeting residents throughout the year in my journey to become a Councillor at the next local elections.

Born in Petts Wood, Bromley, Ian Stotesbury attended Ravenswood School before studying Physics and Astrophysics at the University of Birmingham. He grew up in a safe Conservative seat before moving to Birmingham where he first experienced active campaigns, outraged at the complacency so often seen in safe seats. The motivation for Ian to enter politics is to represent Watford as a hardworking but above all approachable MP for its constituents and be a voice for evidence based policy. He believes in government transparency and openness. He pledges to work tirelessly for Watford and the UK’s interest and set a new standard for approachability and accountability in parliament with highly regular updates of ongoing work, policy decisions and surgeries.

profile.statement_to_voters:
Denise has represented Nelson ward for the past eight years. She wants to continue to represent local residents and stand up for her community. Denise has successfully campaigned for a 20mph speed limit on a number of Nelson streets and plans to continue this campaign until the whole ward has a 20mph limit. Denise has also fought for the right of residents to maintain grass tennis courts at Heigham Park and for a community group to look after the courts. She believes that the city council should use the money it plans to spend installing more modern tennis courts should be spent, instead, on maintaining children's playgrounds in other parts of the city.

Denise has been a staunch supporter of local shops at Earlham House, dealing with a number of issues to do with the building and its environs over the years. She is hoping that recent government legislation can be used to make the company that has been handing out harsh fines to shoppers act more responsibly.

On a city-wide level, Denise is keen to see Norwich take its fair share of action in the battle against climate change, consistent with the Paris Agreement. She has proposed motions to the city council to create a strategy to adapt to the effects climate change and to declare a climate emergency. Her work in this area has been very important. There is still much to be done to persuade the Labour administration of the importance of these issues.

Denise's background in planning and transport has been useful in her life as a city councillor. She has served on the council's Scrutiny and Planning committees, as well as committees dealing with transport and planning issues, Norwich Highways Agency Committee and the Sustainable Development Panel. She is currently the Leader of the Green Group, and the Leader of the Opposition on the city council.

Denise has lived in Nelson ward for 35 years, along with her husband and their cats.

question: Who will win the Central & West Bank ward in the Halton local election on 2026-05-07?

profile.statement_to_voters:
Zack Ali got married in 2009 and made Crawley his home. Since then, he has been a resident of Broadfield. Zack is a proud father of two young boys, both of whom went to Hilltop Primary School in Southgate.  He has received an honours degree in Business management and finance from University of Surrey.

In 2007 he worked for Domino’s Pizza and quickly ended up managing several stores across Sussex and Surrey. He was based at the store on Crawley High Street and led a team of store managers from across the region and was responsible for more than 200 staff. The Crawley store became one of the top 10 performing stores worldwide, whilst under Zack’s leadership. He went on to start his own business, this grew it to the point where it employed over 50 people and served thousands of customers every year.

Zack was elected to Crawley Borough Council and West Sussex County Council in 2021, representing Southgate and Gossops Green division of Crawley. He has sat on several committees and is a member of the shadow cabinet for Planning and Economic development on Crawley Borough Council.

He has been actively involved in Crawley politics since 2007, having worked alongside Henry Smith MP on various local issues and campaigns. Zack has also played a key role in several general election campaigns supporting Henry Smith MP to win the Crawley seat.

He has worked with a number local and national charities in support of a range charitable causes and issues. Including during the pandemic, where he gathered several likeminded people to set up a group to support vulnerable local residents. This group did grocery and food shopping for shielding families, vulnerable households and those on low incomes.

Zack also joined the NHS Volunteer Responders and helped local communities across Crawley to stay safe and feel supported. Taking part in activities such as welfare phone calls with residents living alone and dropping of medication and care packages.

Robin has lived in Crawley for fifty-four years. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of the people who actually live here.

Professionally, Robin works as a Head of Technology, leading teams to build things that serve people rather than impress them. His experience includes:

Leading travel and holiday technology teams for over a decade, making complex systems into travel booking systems in local Crawley companies.
Three years volunteering with the Samaritans — learning to listen.
Active involvement in local clubs and community life across Crawley (On the committee of a local Crawley social sports group).

“I’m a professional. I believe in business, in growth, in making things work. But I’ve never believed those things have to come at the expense of where we live or how we treat each other. Crawley has given me fifty-four years of life. I want to give something back that’s worth having. The Green Party, to me, isn’t about ideology. It’s about the simple idea that every decision — economic, environment  and social — should start by asking what it costs the place and the people around us. That feels like common sense to me. It’s what I’d want from anyone representing me.”

My career has been in software engineering. I've worked in events media, in finance and currently in defence where I manage a project, architect the solutions and write the code. I've spent the last 20 years facing complex problems and solving them. I have been living in Crawley since 2016 and lived in Surrey before that.

I voted Leave in the 2016 referendum. I've never regretted that vote nor the following times we had to hammer the point home to our political class. Like so many others I voted for Boris in the hope that we had an optimistic, British patriot who would champion our people, our country and our values and reverse the downward trends we all experienced. Like so many others I have now seen what the Conservative party has become.

I will never give up on Britain, her values, people and history. With a strong reform of our institutions, we will save Britain.

My priorities are focussed on public safety, the safety of women and girls and to stop the rampant crime that is happening through Crawley.  Spending is out of control and that needs to be brought back to sensible levels and only on what benefits the good people of Crawley.  There are a myriad of other issues to resolve as well, such as restoring the high street, get council house repairs done as close to first time as possible, stop West of Ifield expansion and resist the illegal immigrant settlement into our communities putting our safety at great risk.  None of us at Reform are single issue, we hear all perspectives and record all problems raised to us for addressing.

Oh, and fix the damn potholes.
